intro: Every Indian state and union territory runs its own startup policy on top of the central schemes — seed grants, sustenance allowances, patent and marketing reimbursements, SGST refunds, lease-rental and power subsidies, and preferential public procurement. State benefits stack with central ones. This index gathers the concrete schemes and incentives each state offers, straight from its official startup policy — kept separate from the central playbook.

states.policy: Andaman & Nicobar Islands Innovation and Startup Policy 2018 (operationalised via the A & N Islands Startup Funding and Incentives Scheme 2019)
states.nodalAgency: Directorate of Industries, Andaman and Nicobar Administration
states.summary: A&N was the first UT with a dedicated startup policy (2018), delivered through a detailed funding and incentives scheme (2019) offering monthly sustenance allowances, growth grants, subsidies and reimbursements to recognized startups.

schemes.name: Startup Monthly Allowance (Sustenance)
schemes.benefit: Rs 15,000/month (Rs 20,000/month for women/SC/ST/SEBC/PH founders) for 1 year
schemes.description: Monthly sustenance allowance to support founders during the early stage.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible recognized startups; higher rate for women/reserved-category founders

schemes.name: Startup Growth Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 3 lakh (one-time)
schemes.description: One-time grant to help startups develop their product/service.
schemes.eligibility: Startups meeting eligibility criteria

schemes.name: Marketing Assistance
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 3 lakh (one-time)
schemes.description: One-time support for marketing and publicity expenditure.
schemes.eligibility: DPIIT-recognized startups

schemes.name: Subsidized Incubation / Co-working
schemes.benefit: 50% subsidy, max Rs 5,000/month for 1 year
schemes.description: Subsidy on incubation or co-working space rental.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups

schemes.name: Power Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 50% subsidy, max Rs 5 lakh per annum for 3 years
schemes.description: Subsidy on power/electricity costs for eligible startups.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups

schemes.name: GST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 3 lakh per year for 3 years
schemes.description: Reimbursement of goods and services tax paid by the startup.
schemes.eligibility: DPIIT-recognized startups

schemes.name: Patent Cost Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Full reimbursement of filing/prosecution costs
schemes.description: Reimbursement of patent filing and prosecution expenses.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups

schemes.name: Digital Technology Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 50% of capital expenditure, max Rs 1 lakh
schemes.description: Reimbursement of half the capital cost of computers and related hardware.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups

states.nodalAgency: Andhra Pradesh Innovation Society (APIS), Department of Information Technology, Electronics & Communications
states.summary: Andhra Pradesh runs its startup ecosystem through the AP Innovation Society (APIS), offering phased grants, equity-based seed funding, and a range of reimbursements (SGST, patents, marketing, interest) to registered startups and student innovators.

schemes.name: Startup Grants (Idea to Product Viability)
schemes.benefit: Upfront initial grant up to Rs.2 Lakhs; further grant up to Rs.15 Lakhs (total capped at Rs.15 Lakhs)
schemes.description: Converts innovative ideas into a proof of concept and then supports the startup phase-wise up to product viability. Disbursed in three installments (15%/30%/55%) based on stage and performance.
schemes.eligibility: Students and startups approved under APISP by the Monitoring and Evaluation Committee (MEC)

schemes.name: Grants for Women/BC/SC/ST/Minority/Differently Abled led startups
schemes.benefit: Grant up to Rs.20 Lakhs per startup
schemes.description: Enhanced phase-wise grant for startups with a founder from Women, BC, SC, ST, Minority, Differently Abled and other underrepresented groups, given until product viability is reached.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with a founder from Women/BC/SC/ST/Minority/Differently Abled & Others categories

schemes.name: Seed Funding with Equity
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs.50 Lakhs, based on equity-sharing model
schemes.description: Seed capital investment to help a startup develop its idea and business to the point of attracting further investment.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups approved under APISP

schemes.name: Go-to-Market Support
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs.50 Lakhs, based on equity-sharing model
schemes.description: Funding to help startups commercialize and take their products to market.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups approved under APISP

schemes.name: Need-Based Seed Funding for Deeptech & Advanced Tech Startups
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs.1 Crore, plus 100% rental subsidy for workstations for 30 employees for one year in notified co-working/neighborhood-work/MSME parks
schemes.description: Larger need-based seed funding plus workspace rental subsidy targeted at deeptech and advanced technology startups.
schemes.eligibility: Deeptech and advanced technology startups approved under APISP

schemes.name: SGST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% reimbursement of Net SGST accrued to the state from the startup for 5 years
schemes.description: Refund of net State GST paid on goods produced by the startup, claimed within six months after completion of the financial year.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups under APISP (capped at 100% of eligible fixed capital investment)

schemes.name: Interest Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 8% of term loan due reimbursed
schemes.description: Reimbursement of interest cost up to 8% on the balance term loan taken by the startup.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups under APISP with a term loan

schemes.name: Patent Filing Cost Reimbursement (IPR)
schemes.benefit: 50% of patent expenses, up to Rs.2 Lakh for an India patent and Rs.10 Lakh for a foreign patent
schemes.description: Reimbursement of patent filing fees to encourage protection of intellectual property.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups under APISP that have filed patents

schemes.name: Marketing / Fairs & Events Support
schemes.benefit: 75% of cost, up to Rs.3 Lakhs (max 1 event per year per startup)
schemes.description: Support towards registration, travel, event participation, lodging and boarding expenses for attending fairs and events.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups under APISP with an invitation/registration confirmation from the event organizer

schemes.name: Accelerator Program Support
schemes.benefit: 50% of program fee, up to Rs.2 Lakhs per program (maximum 2 programs per startup)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of acceleration program fees on successful completion of the program.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups under APISP

schemes.name: Matching Grant for Incubators/CoEs/Accelerators
schemes.benefit: Up to 100% matching grant under any Government of India scheme, limited to Rs.50 Lakhs
schemes.description: Support for setting up or expanding incubators, Centres of Excellence and accelerators in government universities or educational institutes in the state.
schemes.eligibility: Select new or existing incubators/CoEs/accelerators in government universities or educational institutes

states.nodalAgency: Department of Finance, Planning & Investment (Arunachal Pradesh Innovation and Investment Park, APIIP), Itanagar
states.summary: Arunachal Pradesh's Startup Policy 2022-23 offers a five-year package of grants, reimbursements and procurement support to build the state's startup ecosystem, with dedicated incentives for women-led ventures and support that complements the central Startup India SISFS scheme.

schemes.name: Idea Support Grant
schemes.benefit: Rs. 5,00,000 (top 10 winners); Rs. 4,00,000 (next 40 winners)
schemes.description: One-time grant awarded to selected early-stage ideas to help convert concepts into ventures.
schemes.eligibility: Selected/winning idea-stage entrepreneurs in the state

schemes.name: Proof of Concept / Seed Support
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 20,00,000 (via central SISFS); up to Rs. 10,00,000 as State support for those not covered under SISFS
schemes.description: Grant for validation of proof of concept, prototype development or product trials, up to 100% of eligible capital expenditure, targeting 100 startups over five years.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups needing PoC/prototype validation

schemes.name: R&D Incentive
schemes.benefit: 50% of capital cost, up to Rs. 5,00,000
schemes.description: Reimbursement of a share of capital cost incurred on research and development activities.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible state startups undertaking R&D

schemes.name: Patent Cost Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% up to Rs. 10,000 (Indian patent); up to Rs. 1,00,000 (international patent)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of patent filing and processing costs for domestic and international filings.
schemes.eligibility: Startups filing patents

schemes.name: Quality Certification Support
schemes.benefit: 100% reimbursement, up to Rs. 5,00,000
schemes.description: Reimbursement of costs of obtaining quality/standards certifications.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible state startups

schemes.name: Domicile Incentive
schemes.benefit: Rs. 1,00,000 one-time grant
schemes.description: One-time incentive for domicile/local startups registered in the state.
schemes.eligibility: Domiciled startups of Arunachal Pradesh

schemes.name: Recruitment Incentive
schemes.benefit: Rs. 40,000 per employee (women / differently-abled)
schemes.description: Incentive to encourage startups to recruit women and differently-abled employees.
schemes.eligibility: Startups hiring women or differently-abled staff

schemes.name: Scale-up Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 25,00,000 (75% reimbursement)
schemes.description: Support for scaling operations, including marketing and growth-stage expenditure.
schemes.eligibility: Growth-stage startups

schemes.name: Digital Up-scaling Subsidy
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 10,00,000 per startup
schemes.description: Reimbursement for procurement and upgradation of high-end computers, hardware, software and related digital infrastructure.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible state startups

schemes.name: Purchase / Procurement Preference
schemes.benefit: 20% procurement preference; waiver of prior experience, turnover, EMD and bid-security requirements
schemes.description: Preference and relaxed tender conditions for state startups in government procurement.
schemes.eligibility: Registered state startups bidding for government tenders

schemes.name: Women Entrepreneurship Support
schemes.benefit: Minimum 33% of seed fund reserved for women-led startups; Entrepreneurship Centre for Women with 50% capital grants up to Rs. 2 crore
schemes.description: Reserved seed funding and a dedicated women's entrepreneurship centre to support women founders.
schemes.eligibility: Women-led/women-founded startups

states.nodalAgency: Department of Industries & Commerce, Government of Assam
states.summary: Assam's startup policy supports innovation-driven ventures registered on the state portal (which issues a 'MASI' My Assam Startup ID) and anchored by the state's flagship incubator 'The Nest' in Guwahati, offering sustenance allowance, seed/proof-of-concept grants and a range of reimbursements and subsidies.

schemes.name: Sustenance Allowance
schemes.benefit: Rs. 20,000/month per startup for 1 year (additional Rs. 5,000/month for Women/ST/SC/Divyang-led startups)
schemes.description: Monthly allowance to support founders during the early stage, with preference accorded to women entrepreneurs.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with valid MASI (My Assam Startup ID)

schemes.name: Idea2POC (Idea to Proof of Concept) Grant
schemes.benefit: Rs. 5,00,000 one-time per startup
schemes.description: One-time grant to help develop a product/service from idea stage to proof of concept.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with valid MASI

schemes.name: Seed Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 50,00,000 per startup (inclusive of the Rs. 5,00,000 Idea2POC grant)
schemes.description: Seed funding support for eligible startups to scale product/service development.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with valid MASI, as evaluated by nodal agencies

schemes.name: Patent (IPR) Filing Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% of costs up to Rs. 1 lakh (domestic patent) and up to Rs. 5 lakh (international patent)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of actual patent filing costs to encourage protection of intellectual property.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with valid MASI

schemes.name: Marketing & Promotion Assistance
schemes.benefit: 50% of costs, up to Rs. 5 lakh per startup
schemes.description: Reimbursement of marketing, promotion and related travel costs incurred by the startup.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with valid MASI

schemes.name: GST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 5 lakh per year for 3 years
schemes.description: Annual reimbursement of state GST paid on sales of goods.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with valid MASI

schemes.name: Lease Rental Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 50% of lease rental for 3 years, ceiling Rs. 5 lakh
schemes.description: Reimbursement of office/workspace lease rental to reduce operating costs.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with valid MASI

schemes.name: Power Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 50% subsidy, max Rs. 10 lakh per year for 5 years
schemes.description: Subsidy on power/electricity charges for eligible startups.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with valid MASI

schemes.name: Stamp Duty Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% of stamp duty/transaction fees on first transaction
schemes.description: Reimbursement of stamp duty on the first land/property transaction by the startup.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with valid MASI

schemes.name: Digital Upgradation Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 50% of capital expenditure, max Rs. 1 lakh
schemes.description: Subsidy on capital expenditure incurred for digital upgradation of the startup.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with valid MASI

schemes.name: Recruitment Assistance (Women/Transgender/Differently-abled hiring)
schemes.benefit: Rs. 5,000 per candidate hired, max Rs. 1 lakh over 3 years
schemes.description: Incentive for employing women, transgender or differently-abled persons.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with valid MASI

schemes.name: The Nest - Incubation Support
schemes.benefit: Incubation space, mentoring and startup support at Guwahati
schemes.description: Flagship state startup incubator ('The Nest') providing physical incubation, mentorship and ecosystem support.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups / applicants on the Assam Startup portal

states.nodalAgency: Department of Industries, Government of Bihar (Start-up Support Unit)
states.summary: Bihar Startup Policy 2022 aims to re-ignite the state's startup ecosystem through an interest-free seed fund, enhanced grants for women/SC-ST/differently-abled founders, patent and incubation support, and matching support for angel-funded startups.

schemes.name: Seed Grant (Interest-Free Loan)
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 10 lakh interest-free loan for 10 years
schemes.description: Seed capital provided to validation/early-stage startups as an interest-free loan repayable over 10 years, released against milestones.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered under the Bihar Startup Policy

schemes.name: Additional Support for Women Entrepreneurs
schemes.benefit: +5% additional grant (seed grant of Rs 10.5 lakh)
schemes.description: Women-led startups receive an additional 5% over the standard seed grant amount.
schemes.eligibility: Women-led startups

schemes.name: Additional Support for SC/ST Entrepreneurs
schemes.benefit: +5% additional grant (22% of corpus reserved)
schemes.description: Startups led by Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe founders receive an additional 5%, with 22% of the fund corpus reserved for SC/ST entrepreneurs.
schemes.eligibility: SC/ST-led startups

schemes.name: Additional Support for Differently-Abled Entrepreneurs
schemes.benefit: +15% additional grant (seed grant of Rs 11.5 lakh)
schemes.description: Startups led by differently-abled founders receive an additional 15% over the standard seed grant amount.
schemes.eligibility: Differently-abled founders

schemes.name: Training / Product Enhancement Grant
schemes.benefit: Rs 3 lakh grant
schemes.description: Grant for rigorous training for product enhancement and funding readiness.
schemes.eligibility: Registered startups undergoing acceleration/training

schemes.name: Patent (IPR) Support
schemes.benefit: 100% funding of domestic patents; reimbursement for foreign patents
schemes.description: Full coverage of domestic patent filing costs and reimbursement of expenses for foreign patents.
schemes.eligibility: Registered startups filing patents

schemes.name: Angel Funding Success Fee
schemes.benefit: 2% of investment as success fee
schemes.description: Success fee provided to early-stage startups that raise capital from state-registered angel investors.
schemes.eligibility: Startups raising angel investment

schemes.name: Matching Loan Support
schemes.benefit: Loan matching the investment raised
schemes.description: Government provides a matching loan for startups that raise funds from Angel Groups or Category-I AIFs.
schemes.eligibility: Startups that secure angel/AIF investment

schemes.name: Co-working / Shared Office Space
schemes.benefit: Free co-working space and shared amenities
schemes.description: Access to free shared office space, amenities and developing co-working spaces for startups.
schemes.eligibility: Registered startups

schemes.name: Free Valuation Services
schemes.benefit: Complimentary valuation services
schemes.description: Free valuation services provided to startups at the commercialization stage.
schemes.eligibility: Registered startups at commercialization stage

schemes.name: Incubator Support
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 2 lakh reimbursement per incubatee; 1:1 matching grants
schemes.description: Incubators receive reimbursement up to Rs 2 lakh per incubatee, a 2-5% fiscal incentive on incubatee investments, and matching grants on a 1:1 basis for Government of India funding.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised incubators in Bihar

states.nodalAgency: Department of Industries, Chandigarh Administration
states.summary: Chandigarh has a dedicated Startup Policy 2025 (five-year cycle) with a grant-in-aid seed fund and staged financial support administered by the Department of Industries, targeting 200+ startups over five years.

schemes.name: Idea Stage Grant (Grand Competition)
schemes.benefit: Rs 2 lakh for top 20 startups; Rs 1 lakh for next 50 startups
schemes.description: Early idea-stage grants awarded through a startup grand competition to help validate and develop ideas.
schemes.eligibility: Early idea-stage startups selected via competition

schemes.name: Seed Stage Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 7 lakh per startup; additional Rs 2 lakh for women/transgender-led startups
schemes.description: Grant-in-aid seed funding to support product development and early operations.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized seed-stage startups

schemes.name: Early-Stage Commercialisation Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 12 lakh per startup; additional Rs 2 lakh for women/transgender-led startups
schemes.description: Grant to support commercialisation and scaling of validated products/services.
schemes.eligibility: Early-stage startups moving to commercialisation

schemes.name: Rental Subsidy
schemes.benefit: Rs 5,000/month for 12 months
schemes.description: Rental support for startups using recognized co-working spaces.
schemes.eligibility: Startups using recognized co-working spaces

schemes.name: Incubation Support
schemes.benefit: Free incubation facilities for one year
schemes.description: Free access to incubation facilities; administration also partners with HEIs to set up or upgrade incubators.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups

schemes.name: Patent and Certification Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Patent support up to Rs 10,000 (80% of cost); certification support up to Rs 1 lakh (80% of cost)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of a share of IP filing and quality certification costs.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups

states.policy: Chhattisgarh Innovation and Startup Promotion Policy 2025-30
states.nodalAgency: Department of Commerce & Industries, Government of Chhattisgarh (state incubator: 36Inc)
states.summary: Chhattisgarh's 2025-30 startup policy is a sector-agnostic framework supporting recognized startups from ideation to scale-up through seed funding, a Rs.100 crore Startup Capital Fund, collateral-free credit, and a range of subsidies and reimbursements. Implementation is led by the Department of Commerce & Industries with the state-backed 36Inc incubator in Raipur.

schemes.name: Seed Funding (Proof of Concept / MVP)
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 10 lakh
schemes.description: Grant/seed funding of up to Rs. 10 lakh for startups with a validated Proof of Concept to develop a Minimum Viable Product (MVP).
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups with a validated Proof of Concept

schemes.name: Startup Capital Fund
schemes.benefit: Rs. 100 crore corpus
schemes.description: A Rs. 100 crore Startup Capital Fund deployed through SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds to provide equity/scale-up capital to startups.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups seeking growth/scale-up capital

schemes.name: Credit Risk Fund / Collateral-Free Loans
schemes.benefit: Collateral-free loans up to Rs. 1 crore (Rs. 50 crore fund)
schemes.description: A Rs. 50 crore Credit Risk Fund provides credit guarantees enabling startup units to access collateral-free bank loans of up to Rs. 1 crore.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startup units

schemes.name: Interest Subsidy
schemes.benefit: Up to 75% for 5 years, max Rs. 50 lakh
schemes.description: Interest subsidy of up to 75% for a period of five years on term loans or working capital, subject to a maximum of Rs. 50 lakh.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups with term/working capital loans

schemes.name: Patent / IPR Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 75% of cost; max Rs. 10 lakh (national), Rs. 20 lakh (international)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of 75% of expenses incurred in obtaining a patent, up to Rs. 10 lakh for national patents and up to Rs. 20 lakh for international patents.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups filing patents

schemes.name: Lease / Rental Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 50% of rent, max Rs. 15,000/month for 3 years
schemes.description: 50% assistance on monthly rent/lease of workspace for up to three years, subject to a maximum of Rs. 15,000 per month, including space at incubation centres.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups in leased/rented or incubation premises

schemes.name: EPF Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 75% of EPF contribution, max Rs. 1 crore/year for 5 years
schemes.description: Reimbursement of 75% of the employer's EPF contribution for skilled and semi-skilled employees of Chhattisgarh domicile for up to five years from start of commercial production, capped at Rs. 1 crore per year.
schemes.eligibility: Startups employing Chhattisgarh-domicile skilled/semi-skilled workers

schemes.name: Employment / Wage Incentive
schemes.benefit: Rs. 6,000/month per female, Rs. 5,000/month per male employee
schemes.description: Startups creating more than 10 permanent jobs receive assistance of up to Rs. 6,000 per month per female employee and Rs. 5,000 per month per male employee.
schemes.eligibility: Startups providing more than 10 permanent jobs

schemes.name: Special Category Wage Subsidy
schemes.benefit: Up to 40% wage subsidy for 5 years
schemes.description: Enhanced wage subsidy of up to 40% for five years for employing specially-abled persons, retired Agniveers, and Naxalism-affected individuals.
schemes.eligibility: Startups employing special-category workers

schemes.name: Marketing / Event Participation Support
schemes.benefit: 50% grant on event costs; digital advertising reimbursement
schemes.description: 50% grant on total expenditure (travel, registration, stall/booth charges) for participation in national and international startup events, plus reimbursement of digital/online advertising expenses.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups participating in startup events

schemes.name: Quality Certification & Technology Support
schemes.benefit: Support for quality certification, patents and technology acquisition
schemes.description: Assistance towards quality certifications, technology purchase/acquisition and related standards compliance for startups (quantum as notified under policy guidelines).
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups

schemes.name: SGST Reimbursement & Stamp Duty Exemption
schemes.benefit: Net SGST reimbursement; stamp duty exemption on land/building
schemes.description: Reimbursement of net SGST paid by eligible startups along with stamp duty exemption on land and building documents, aligned with the state Industrial Development Policy.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startup units

schemes.name: Government Procurement Relaxation
schemes.benefit: Relaxation of procurement norms for startups
schemes.description: Relaxation of government procurement norms (such as prior turnover and experience conditions) to enable startups to supply to state government departments.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups bidding for government tenders

schemes.name: Incubation Support (36Inc & Incubators)
schemes.benefit: Incubator establishment grant 40% of cost, up to Rs. 40 lakh; annual operational support
schemes.description: Support for incubators including establishment grants of 40% of expenses (up to Rs. 40 lakh) and annual operational support, with mentorship, co-working space and fund support delivered through the state 36Inc incubator.
schemes.eligibility: Incubators, academic institutions and startups

states.state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
states.nodalAgency: District Industries Centre, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
states.summary: DNHDD has no separate stand-alone startup policy; support runs through the industrial Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) 2022-2027 and its reimbursement schemes, alongside the national Startup India initiative. Startup-specific monetary caps are not published on the official portal.

schemes.name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) 2022-2027
schemes.description: Umbrella industrial incentive scheme (2022-2027) administered via the Single Window / NSWS portal, offering fiscal incentives and multiple reimbursement schemes to industrial units including MSMEs and startups.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible industrial units/enterprises registered in the UT

states.policy: Draft Delhi Startup Policy, 2025 (successor to the Cabinet-approved Delhi Startup Policy, 2022)
states.nodalAgency: Department of Industries, Govt. of NCT of Delhi (implemented via a Policy Monitoring Committee chaired by the Commissioner of Industries and a Startup Task Force)
states.summary: Delhi's startup framework offers fiscal and non-fiscal support including a monthly sustenance allowance, lease-rental and patent reimbursements, exhibition/marketing assistance and a proposed VC fund, delivered through the Department of Industries and a dedicated Delhi Startup Portal. The 2025 policy is still in draft (public comments were sought Aug-Sep 2025); it builds on the Delhi Startup Policy 2022 approved by the Cabinet in May 2022.

schemes.name: Sustenance (Operational) Allowance
schemes.benefit: Monthly allowance of Rs 2 lakh for up to one year
schemes.description: Recurring monthly allowance to cover operational/running expenses of early-stage startups.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Delhi startups (early-stage)

schemes.name: Delhi Startup Venture Capital Fund
schemes.benefit: Proposed Rs 200 crore fund
schemes.description: A state venture capital fund to provide early-stage backing to high-potential startups while encouraging private co-investment.
schemes.eligibility: Early-stage, high-potential recognised startups

schemes.name: Collateral-free Loan Support
schemes.benefit: Collateral-free loans (up to Rs 20 lakh referenced under the central CGTMSE scheme)
schemes.description: Facilitation of collateral-free credit for startups, leveraging central credit-guarantee support.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Delhi startups

schemes.name: Patent / IPR Filing Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 1 lakh for domestic patents and up to Rs 3 lakh for international patents
schemes.description: Reimbursement of costs incurred by startups in filing domestic and international patents.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Delhi startups filing patents

schemes.name: Marketing / Exhibition Assistance (Market Development Assistance)
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 5 lakh for domestic events and up to Rs 10 lakh for international events
schemes.description: Reimbursement of participation, stall and travel costs for domestic and international exhibitions and startup events.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Delhi startups participating in approved events

schemes.name: Lease Rental / Co-working Space Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% reimbursement of lease rentals up to Rs 10 lakh per year for three years
schemes.description: Full reimbursement of co-working / office lease rentals to reduce workspace costs for startups.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Delhi startups leasing eligible workspace

schemes.name: Women-led Startup Special Incentives
schemes.benefit: Enhanced exhibition reimbursement ceilings (about Rs 5.5 lakh domestic / Rs 11 lakh international once a year), plus priority access and mentorship
schemes.description: Higher reimbursement ceilings, priority access to benefits and curated mentorship from industry leaders, CAs and legal experts for women-led startups and marginalised communities.
schemes.eligibility: Women-led startups and startups from marginalised communities

schemes.name: Delhi Incubation Hub & Incubation Support
schemes.benefit: Virtual incubation services plus new incubation centres and co-working spaces with subsidies (up to five years)
schemes.description: A Delhi Incubation Hub offering mentorship, expert and industry-network access, and physical incubation/co-working infrastructure with capital and operational subsidies.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Delhi startups

schemes.name: Delhi Startup Portal & Free Professional Services
schemes.benefit: Single-window portal plus free consultancy (legal, CA, marketing, taxation, fundraising)
schemes.description: A digital single-window portal for registration, incentive applications, mentor-matching and grievance redressal, supported by a panel of lawyers, CAs and experts providing free-of-cost services to startups.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Delhi startups

states.nodalAgency: Start-up Promotion Cell (SITPC), Department of Information Technology, Electronics & Communications, Government of Goa
states.summary: Goa's IT Start-up Policy offers technology-based startups a package of grants and reimbursements covering seed capital, rent, salaries, internet/cloud costs, IPR and R&D, administered through the Start-up Promotion Cell (SITPC) under the state IT Department.

schemes.name: Seed Capital Scheme
schemes.benefit: One-time grant of up to INR 10 lakh
schemes.description: One-time seed grant for promising startups, with up to 100 startups selected each year by the SITPC.
schemes.eligibility: SITPC-certified startups with Aadhaar-linked director bank accounts

schemes.name: Lease Rental Subsidy (Leased/Privately Owned Premises Scheme)
schemes.benefit: INR 20 per sq ft per month, capped at INR 3 lakh per annum, for up to 2 years
schemes.description: Reimbursement of lease rent for local startups operating from rented premises, reimbursed quarterly; up to 25 startups selected per year.
schemes.eligibility: SITPC-certified local startups not using government co-working facilities

schemes.name: Internet, Software & Cloud Cost Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 1 lakh per quarter for up to 1 year
schemes.description: Reimbursement of internet connectivity, software license fees and cloud service fees for startups operating from own or rented premises; up to 20 startups per year.
schemes.eligibility: SITPC-certified startups not using government co-working spaces

schemes.name: Salary Reimbursement Scheme
schemes.benefit: 50% of fresher salary up to INR 15,000/month per employee; plus up to 25% of local workforce salary capped at INR 25 lakh/year
schemes.description: Reimbursement to encourage hiring of local talent and freshers, for up to 25 employees over 3 years.
schemes.eligibility: SITPC-certified startups; employees hired for a minimum of 12 months with Aadhaar-linked accounts

schemes.name: IPR Reimbursement Scheme
schemes.benefit: 100% of costs up to INR 2 lakh (national IP) or INR 5 lakh (international IP)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of government and attorney fees for filing technology-based patents/IP; up to 200 startups per year.
schemes.eligibility: SITPC-certified startups with technology-based IP; one-time payment per IP application

schemes.name: Trademark Reimbursement Scheme
schemes.benefit: 50% of trademark registration cost, up to INR 25,000
schemes.description: Reimbursement of trademark registration costs upon successful registration; up to 200 applicants per year.
schemes.eligibility: SITPC-certified startups with successful trademark registration

schemes.name: R&D Reimbursement Scheme
schemes.benefit: 50% of R&D expenses up to INR 5 lakh/year (salary component capped at INR 2 lakh), for 2 years
schemes.description: Reimbursement of research and development expenses including salaries of PhD/Master degree holders; up to 100 startups per year.
schemes.eligibility: SITPC-certified local startups with a working prototype and qualified staff hired 12+ months

schemes.name: Co-Working Spaces/Incubators/Accelerators Subsidy Scheme
schemes.benefit: 50% of seat cost: up to INR 3,000/seat (co-working, 2 yrs), INR 5,000/seat (incubator, 1 yr), INR 6,000/seat (accelerator, 3 months), max 8 seats
schemes.description: Subsidy on seat/desk costs at co-working spaces, incubators and accelerators; deserving startups may get up to 100% reimbursement.
schemes.eligibility: SITPC-certified startups using digital payments and Aadhaar-linked bank accounts

schemes.name: Grant for Incubation Centres within Educational Institutes
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 10 lakh one-time capital grant plus INR 3 lakh/year operational support for 3 years
schemes.description: Grants to set up and run incubation centres within educational institutes; up to 15 institutes per year for capital support.
schemes.eligibility: Educational institutes affiliated with recognised public universities

schemes.name: Technology Fellowship and Patent Promotion Scheme
schemes.benefit: Fellowship of up to INR 10 lakh per year and patent grant of up to INR 15 lakh
schemes.description: Supports technology educators through training/summer schools at Centres of Excellence and funds business and value-driven patents.
schemes.eligibility: Technology educators/faculty in Goa as certified by SITPC

states.policy: Scheme for Assistance for Startups/Innovation (under Gujarat Industrial Policy 2020)
states.nodalAgency: Industries Commissionerate, Government of Gujarat
states.summary: Gujarat supports startups mainly through the Scheme for Assistance for Startups/Innovation (Industrial Policy 2020), offering a monthly sustenance allowance, seed support, IPR and skill-development assistance and incubation grants, complemented by the Student Startup & Innovation Policy (SSIP 2.0) for student innovators.

schemes.name: Sustenance Allowance
schemes.benefit: INR 20,000/month for 1 year (INR 25,000/month if at least one woman co-founder)
schemes.description: Monthly allowance paid to approved startups to help founders sustain themselves during the early development phase.
schemes.eligibility: Startups/innovators approved under the Scheme for Assistance for Startups/Innovation, recommended by a nodal institution

schemes.name: Seed Support
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 30 lakh
schemes.description: Assistance toward cost of raw materials, consumables, hardware/software, professional services and other tools required for prototype/product/process development, testing and marketing.
schemes.eligibility: Approved startups under the Scheme for Assistance for Startups/Innovation

schemes.name: Assistance for Social Impact Startups
schemes.benefit: Additional up to INR 10 lakh
schemes.description: Extra assistance for startups generating significant positive impact on society, over and above the standard seed support.
schemes.eligibility: Startups demonstrating significant social impact

schemes.name: Skill Development / Soft Skills Training Assistance
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 1 lakh per startup
schemes.description: Support to cover the cost of soft-skills and capability-building training for startup founders/teams.
schemes.eligibility: Approved startups under the Scheme for Assistance for Startups/Innovation

schemes.name: IPR / Patent Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 2 lakh (domestic patent) / up to INR 10 lakh (international patent)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of costs incurred by startups in filing and obtaining patents and other intellectual property rights.
schemes.eligibility: Approved startups under the Scheme for Assistance for Startups/Innovation

schemes.name: Acceleration Program Assistance
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 3 lakh
schemes.description: Reimbursement to enable startups to participate in a nationally or internationally recognised acceleration program.
schemes.eligibility: Approved startups under the Scheme for Assistance for Startups/Innovation

schemes.name: Assistance to Incubators / Nodal Institutions
schemes.benefit: Capital grant up to 50% (max INR 50 lakh); mentoring assistance up to INR 5 lakh/year
schemes.description: Support to recognised institutions to set up and operate incubation infrastructure and to provide mentoring to startups.
schemes.eligibility: Universities, educational bodies, incubators, PSUs and R&D institutions recognised as nodal institutions

schemes.name: Student Startup & Innovation Policy (SSIP 2.0)
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 2.5 lakh per PoC/prototype (INR 20,000 for school students up to Class 12); IPR support up to 100% of cost
schemes.description: Education Department scheme funding student proof-of-concepts, prototypes and innovations, plus IPR support for patents, copyrights and trademarks.
schemes.eligibility: Students (school to doctoral) and alumni of Gujarat-based institutions, up to age 35, via participating institutes

schemes.name: Pre-Series A Funding (Gujarat Venture Finance Ltd)
schemes.benefit: INR 50 lakh to INR 3 crore
schemes.description: Mid-stage/pre-Series A equity financing for growth-stage startups through a dedicated fund managed by Gujarat Venture Finance Limited.
schemes.eligibility: Growth-stage startups meeting the fund's investment criteria

states.nodalAgency: Department of Industries and Commerce, Haryana (Startup Haryana Cell)
states.summary: Haryana's 2022 startup policy offers DPIIT-recognised, Startup Haryana-registered ventures a package of fiscal benefits including seed grants, lease-rental subsidy, patent-cost and net-SGST reimbursement, and cloud/acceleration support, with enhanced rates for women-led startups.

schemes.name: Seed Grant / Seed Funding for Startups
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 10 Lakh per startup
schemes.description: Seed grant to help startups validate ideas, build prototypes, demonstrate proof of concept, and undertake market research, skill training and initial marketing.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered with Startup Haryana; allocation by block category (100 in 'A', 250 in 'B', 750 in 'C', 1000 in 'D' blocks)

schemes.name: Lease Rental Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 30% of lease rental (45% for women-only founder startups), max INR 5 Lakh for 1 year
schemes.description: Reimbursement of lease rental for startups operating from incubators, IT Parks, industrial clusters or other notified locations.
schemes.eligibility: Registered startups operating from eligible premises; enhanced rate for startups with only women founders

schemes.name: Patent Cost Reimbursement (IPR)
schemes.benefit: 100% of actual expenses, maximum INR 25 Lakh
schemes.description: Reimburses patent filing, consultancy, search, maintenance and publishing fees for domestic and international patents.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered with Startup Haryana

schemes.name: Net SGST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 50% Net SGST reimbursement for 7 years, capped at 100% of Fixed Capital Investment
schemes.description: Reimbursement of net State GST paid, to reduce tax burden on eligible startups.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered with Startup Haryana

schemes.name: Cloud Storage / Hosting Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 75% reimbursement up to INR 2.5 Lakh per startup per annum for 5 years
schemes.description: Reimbursement of cloud computing and storage expenses incurred on Haryana-based data centres.
schemes.eligibility: Registered startups using Haryana-based data centres

schemes.name: Acceleration Program Assistance
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 2.5 Lakh annually
schemes.description: Assistance towards fees for participation in national acceleration programs.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered with Startup Haryana

states.policy: Chief Minister's Startup/Innovation Projects/New Industries Scheme, 2016 (amended)
states.nodalAgency: Department of Industries, Government of Himachal Pradesh
states.summary: Himachal Pradesh's flagship startup scheme aims to turn educated youth from job seekers into job creators by supporting innovation projects and startups with a monthly sustenance allowance, free incubation, patent and marketing assistance, and fiscal incentives such as interest subvention and concessional land/stamp duty.
sectors: Rural infrastructure, crafts, water and sanitation

schemes.name: Sustenance Allowance
schemes.benefit: Rs. 25,000 per month for one year
schemes.description: Monthly sustenance allowance paid for one year to approved innovation projects recommended by the host/incubation institution and cleared by the Empowered Committee.
schemes.eligibility: Individuals/groups with innovative ideas whose projects are recommended by a host institution and approved by the Empowered Committee.

schemes.name: Free Incubation Support
schemes.benefit: Free mentoring, lab and facility access
schemes.description: Recognised incubation centres provide mentoring services and free-of-cost access to their labs and facilities to startups and innovation projects.
schemes.eligibility: Startups and innovation projects hosted at an approved incubation centre.

schemes.name: Marketing / Commercialization Assistance
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 10 lakh
schemes.description: Support for marketing and commercialization of products/services once the idea is ready for the market, on recommendation of the incubation centre.
schemes.eligibility: Approved startups/innovation projects recommended for commercialization by the incubation centre.

schemes.name: Patent (IPR) Filing Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 2 lakh (Indian patent); up to Rs. 10 lakh (foreign patent)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of patent filing costs, disbursed in stages (75% on filing, 25% on grant), up to Rs. 2 lakh for an Indian patent and up to Rs. 10 lakh (or actual cost, whichever is lower) for a foreign patent on a single subject.
schemes.eligibility: Approved startups/innovation projects filing patents.

schemes.name: Interest Subvention
schemes.benefit: 5% interest subvention for three years
schemes.description: Interest subvention of 5% for micro-sector startups on eligible loans/investment (up to Rs. 25 lakh) for a period of three years.
schemes.eligibility: Micro-sector startups with investment up to Rs. 25 lakh.

schemes.name: Concessional Land Allotment
schemes.benefit: Land at 50% of standard rates
schemes.description: Allotment of land to eligible startups at concessional rates of 50% of the standard/prevailing rate.
schemes.eligibility: Approved startups under the scheme.

schemes.name: Stamp Duty Concession
schemes.benefit: Stamp duty reduced to 3% on conveyance and lease deeds
schemes.description: Reduced stamp duty on conveyance and lease deeds for startup units under the scheme.
schemes.eligibility: Approved startups under the scheme.

schemes.name: Incubator Setup Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 30 lakh per year for three years
schemes.description: Financial support to establish and run recognised incubation centres, plus recurring expense support of up to Rs. 10 lakh per year.
schemes.eligibility: Institutions setting up approved incubation centres under the scheme.

schemes.name: Incubator Lease Rental Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Rs. 5 per sq.ft. per month or 25% of actual rent (whichever is less), for three years
schemes.description: Reimbursement of rental costs to recognised incubators for a period of three years.
schemes.eligibility: Approved incubation centres under the scheme.

states.nodalAgency: Jammu and Kashmir Entrepreneurship Development Institute (JKEDI), under the Industries & Commerce Department, J&K
states.summary: J&K's revised startup policy (superseding the 2018 policy) aims to establish 2,000 startups by 2027 through seed funding, a venture capital fund, incubation support, mentorship, patent assistance and special support for women and student entrepreneurs, implemented by JKEDI.

schemes.name: Startup Seed Fund Scheme
schemes.benefit: One-time assistance up to Rs 20 lakh (in 4 equal installments)
schemes.description: Financial assistance to JKEDI-recognised startups for prototype/product development, marketing and scaling of innovative products.
schemes.eligibility: Startup recognised by JKEDI and approved by the Startup Task Force Committee

schemes.name: J&K Startup Venture Capital Fund
schemes.benefit: Rs 250 crore fund (Rs 25 crore initial infusion by J&K Administration)
schemes.description: A venture capital fund to invest in and scale up J&K startups, drawing further contributions from private entities and high net-worth individuals.
schemes.eligibility: J&K-based recognised startups

schemes.name: Incubation Support Grant
schemes.benefit: Capital grant of Rs 200 lakh to selected incubators (up to Rs 50 lakh each)
schemes.description: Grant to establish and empower incubation centres to provide infrastructure, mentoring and handholding to startups.
schemes.eligibility: Selected incubators / incubation centres in J&K

schemes.name: Co-working / Office Space Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 50% discount on office space for 1 year (focus-sector startups); free space for Section 8 SDG firms
schemes.description: Subsidised co-working space at JKEDI campuses (Pampore, Bari-Brahmana), with free space for Section 8 companies working on sustainable development goals.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups, focus-sector/Section 8 firms

schemes.name: Patent / IPR Assistance
schemes.description: Patent-related assistance and facilitation for recognised startups to protect intellectual property.
schemes.eligibility: JKEDI-recognised startups

schemes.name: Mentorship Financial Assistance
schemes.description: Financial assistance for mentorship, acceleration programmes, networking and exposure visits for recognised startups.
schemes.eligibility: JKEDI-recognised startups

schemes.name: Women Entrepreneur Special Support
schemes.description: Additional need-based support to female entrepreneurs to promote gender-inclusive startup growth.
schemes.eligibility: Women-led startups in J&K

schemes.name: Annual Startup Awards
schemes.benefit: Cash recognition (e.g. Top Startup of the Year Rs 50,000)
schemes.description: Annual awards recognising top startups and incubation centres in the Union Territory.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised J&K startups and incubators

schemes.name: Public Procurement Relaxation & Self-Certification
schemes.benefit: Relaxed public procurement norms and self-certification under labour/environment laws
schemes.description: Eases market access and compliance burden for recognised startups in government procurement.
schemes.eligibility: JKEDI/DPIIT-recognised startups

schemes.name: Youth Start-up Loan Scheme (YSLS)
schemes.benefit: Bank credit facility up to Rs 8 lakh
schemes.description: JKEDI-implemented soft-loan scheme (under SKEWPY) enabling unemployed youth to set up start-up enterprises.
schemes.eligibility: Unemployed J&K state subjects aged 18-40 with minimum 10+2 qualification

schemes.name: Seed Capital Fund Scheme (SCFS / SKEWPY)
schemes.benefit: Seed money up to Rs 7.50 lakh with concessional bank finance
schemes.description: Provides seed capital coupled with bank finance at concessional rates to eligible youth to establish enterprises.
schemes.eligibility: Unemployed J&K state subjects aged 18-40 (up to 42 for differently-abled) with 10+2 qualification

states.nodalAgency: Atal Bihari Vajpayee Innovation Lab (ABVIL), Department of Information Technology & e-Governance, Government of Jharkhand
states.summary: Jharkhand's 2023 startup policy, implemented through ABVIL, offers stage-wise support (sustenance allowance and scale-up grants) plus a range of fiscal reimbursements (SGST, patents, lease rentals, marketing) and extra incentives for women, SC/ST, transgender and Divyang founders, with a goal of nurturing 1,000 startups by 2028.

schemes.name: Sustenance/Subsistence Allowance
schemes.benefit: INR 15,000 per member per month (up to 3 members) for 12 months
schemes.description: Monthly stipend for selected startups at the idea/seed/prototype stage, paid for 12 months from the date of selection to support founders while building their product.
schemes.eligibility: Selected individuals/groups at idea, seed or prototype stage

schemes.name: Special Additional Allowance (Women / SC-ST / Transgender / Divyang)
schemes.benefit: Additional INR 2,000 per member per month for 12 months over the sustenance allowance
schemes.description: Extra monthly allowance on top of the sustenance allowance for eligible Jharkhand female, SC/ST, transgender or Divyang (differently-abled) members.
schemes.eligibility: Female, SC/ST, transgender or Divyang members of selected startups

schemes.name: Scale-up Stage Grant
schemes.benefit: One-time grant up to INR 10 lakh
schemes.description: One-time support to help startups scale up their business, product or solution at the secondary/scale-up stage.
schemes.eligibility: Startups moving to the scale-up stage

schemes.name: SGST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% of net SGST paid in cash per annum for 5 years
schemes.description: Reimbursement of the net State GST paid in cash by the unit each year for up to five years.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered in Jharkhand

schemes.name: Patent / IPR Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 50,000 per patent (companies); INR 25,000 per candidate for colleges (max 20/year)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of patent filing fees, provided the patent is filed from a Jharkhand office and granted; colleges running patent-filing courses are reimbursed per passing candidate.
schemes.eligibility: Startups/units and colleges located in Jharkhand

schemes.name: Lease Rental Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 50% of lease rentals, max INR 5 lakh per year for 3 years
schemes.description: Reimbursement of office lease rentals for startups leasing space in STPIs, IT/multi-purpose SEZs or IT Parks.
schemes.eligibility: Startups leasing office space in STPI/SEZ/IT Parks

schemes.name: Special Reimbursement Uplift (Women / SC-ST / Divyang)
schemes.benefit: Additional 5% reimbursement on lease rentals, internet bandwidth and electricity bills
schemes.description: SC/ST, female and Divyang startups receive an extra 5% on lease rental, internet bandwidth and electricity reimbursements, subject to the same maximum caps.
schemes.eligibility: SC/ST, female and Divyang startups

schemes.name: Quality Certification Support
schemes.benefit: 100% financial support up to INR 25 lakh
schemes.description: Full support for costs of obtaining quality certification from BIS and internationally recognized institutions.
schemes.eligibility: Registered Jharkhand startups

schemes.name: Stamp Duty & Registration Fee Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% of stamp duty and registration fee on the first transaction
schemes.description: Full reimbursement of stamp duty and registration fee paid on sale/lease deeds, on the first transaction only.
schemes.eligibility: Registered Jharkhand startups

schemes.name: Trade Fair & Marketing Support
schemes.benefit: 75% of stall charges (max INR 4 lakh) and 75% of airfare (up to INR 50,000/person, max 2 per unit per year) for international trade fairs
schemes.description: Support for participation in international trade fairs to help startups market their products globally.
schemes.eligibility: Registered Jharkhand startups

schemes.name: Jharkhand Entrepreneurship Development Fund
schemes.benefit: State fund for startup investment and support
schemes.description: A dedicated state fund established under the policy to channel funding and financial support to startups.
schemes.eligibility: Startups selected under the policy

schemes.name: Incubation & Innovation Infrastructure
schemes.benefit: Central Innovation Lab, incubation centres and incentives for private incubators/accelerators
schemes.description: Setting up a Central Innovation Lab as a networking platform, incubation centres in higher educational institutions, Centers of Excellence, and incentives for private incubators and accelerators.
schemes.eligibility: Startups, institutions and private incubators/accelerators

states.nodalAgency: Karnataka Innovation and Technology Society (KITS) / Karnataka Startup Cell, Department of Electronics, IT, Bt and S&T, Government of Karnataka
states.summary: Karnataka's startup policy aims to nurture up to 25,000 startups across a 9-pillar approach (funding, incubation, infrastructure, mentoring, inclusion), with a strong thrust on emerging clusters Beyond Bengaluru. It offers seed grants, a venture capital fund, and a package of reimbursements and concessions to DPIIT-style recognised startups registered with the Karnataka Startup Cell.

schemes.name: Idea2PoC Grant-in-Aid Scheme (ELEVATE)
schemes.benefit: One-time grant of up to ₹50 lakh
schemes.description: Early-stage seed funding to help startups develop their idea to a proof-of-concept stage; 10% of the fund is earmarked for Deep Tech, Assistive Technology and SDG-related startups, with subsidised incubation support.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered with the Karnataka Startup Cell requiring early-stage seed support to reach PoC.

schemes.name: Startup Funding through Fund of Funds (Venture Capital Fund)
schemes.benefit: ₹100 crore venture capital fund
schemes.description: A state venture capital fund to invest in emerging, innovative and deep-tech startups in AI, ML, electric vehicles, MedTech, robotics, drones and other disruptive technologies across sectors.
schemes.eligibility: Emerging innovative and deep-tech startups; 25% of the fund earmarked for women-led startups.

schemes.name: Beyond Bengaluru Cluster Seed Fund
schemes.benefit: Dedicated seed fund (amount per startup not specified)
schemes.description: Funding support for growth-stage startups established outside Bengaluru Urban District to enable scaling up, dedicated to the emerging technology clusters of Mysuru, Hubballi and Mangalore.
schemes.eligibility: Growth-stage startups established outside Bengaluru Urban District.

schemes.name: Reimbursement of State GST (SGST)
schemes.benefit: 100% reimbursement of annual SGST for first 3 years
schemes.description: Full reimbursement of annual State GST for the first three years of incubation for Beyond Bengaluru startups incubated in Government-supported incubators/CIFs.
schemes.eligibility: Beyond Bengaluru startups incubated in GoK-supported incubators/CIFs with maximum annual turnover of ₹1 crore.

schemes.name: Marketing Cost Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 30% of actual cost, up to ₹5 lakh per year
schemes.description: Reimbursement of 30% of actual costs (including travel) incurred in international marketing through trade show participation.
schemes.eligibility: Registered startups; capped at ₹5 lakh per year per startup.

schemes.name: Patents Filing Cost Reimbursement (IPR)
schemes.benefit: Up to ₹2 lakh per Indian patent; up to ₹10 lakh for foreign patents
schemes.description: Reimbursement of patent filing and prosecution costs, paid 75% after filing and 25% after grant, for startups incubated in GoK-supported incubators/CIFs.
schemes.eligibility: Startups incubated in GoK-supported incubators/CIFs; ₹2 lakh per Indian patent awarded, up to ₹10 lakh for a foreign patent on a single subject matter.

schemes.name: Quality Certification Cost Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 50% of cost, overall ceiling ₹6 lakh
schemes.description: Reimbursement of 50% of quality certification fees for up to 3 industry-standard certifications.
schemes.eligibility: Startups located outside Bengaluru Urban District.

schemes.name: Women Entrepreneurship Support (Elevate WomEN)
schemes.benefit: Direct loan up to ₹10 lakh; 25% of ₹100 cr VC fund earmarked; 20% incubator seats reserved
schemes.description: A package for women-led startups including an exclusive Idea2PoC/seed program for Beyond Bengaluru, the Elevate WomEN acceleration and incubation programme, 20% reserved incubator seats, and a direct loan through the Karnataka State Women Development Corporation.
schemes.eligibility: Entities with women founder/co-founder holding minimum 51% stake (partial benefits for 51% women employment).

schemes.name: Grand Challenges Karnataka (GCK)
schemes.benefit: Initial grant ₹10 lakh + follow-on ₹50 lakh
schemes.description: Challenge-based grants where winning startups receive an initial grant to test/demonstrate a solution to a societal problem and follow-on funding for deployment; 3 challenges announced each year with Government departments/corporates.
schemes.eligibility: Startups proposing solutions to societal/ESG/SDG challenges identified by Government departments.

schemes.name: Experiential Internship Support
schemes.benefit: Up to ₹1 lakh internship subsidy per startup
schemes.description: Subsidises internship fees for social entrepreneurs to hire up to 3 interns (minimum 6 months); available for about 50 startups annually, once during the policy period.
schemes.eligibility: Social-entrepreneur startups hiring interns from Beyond Bengaluru.

schemes.name: Regulatory Sandbox Fee Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 75% of application fees reimbursed to startups
schemes.description: Under the Karnataka Innovation Authority Act 2020, startups participating in a regulatory sandbox get 75% of application fees reimbursed (sandbox operators supporting 5+ startups get 50% of processing fees).
schemes.eligibility: Startups participating in a regulatory sandbox under the KIA Act 2020.

schemes.name: Preferential Market Access in Public Procurement
schemes.benefit: Relaxations and value-based preference in Government tenders
schemes.description: Amended Karnataka Transparency in Public Procurements Rules provide general relaxations for startups, value-based preferential procurement, and adoption of a startup inclusion plan in tenders. Startups may also self-certify under specified labour laws.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered with the Karnataka Startup Cell participating in Government/PSU procurement.

states.nodalAgency: Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM)
states.summary: Kerala Startup Mission is the nodal agency implementing the state's technology startup policy, offering staged innovation grants, soft-loan seed funding, IPR reimbursement, and market, rent and procurement support to technology-based product startups registered in Kerala.

schemes.name: Idea Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 3 lakhs
schemes.description: Milestone-based grant helping early-stage entrepreneurs convert an idea or proof of concept into a working prototype and validate technical and market feasibility.
schemes.eligibility: Kerala-based startups in ideation/PoC stage building an innovative tech product; incorporation (Pvt Ltd/LLP/OPC) and KSUM Unique ID required at disbursal.

schemes.name: Productisation Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 7 lakhs (up to Rs. 12 lakhs for women-led startups)
schemes.description: Grant to convert a minimum viable prototype into a market-ready product, covering testing, validation and early market entry.
schemes.eligibility: Startups in product-development stage, incorporated as Pvt Ltd/LLP with active MCA status, owning an innovative tech product and holding a KSUM Unique ID.

schemes.name: Scale-up Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 15 lakhs
schemes.description: Grant for market-validated startups to expand markets, strengthen branding, enhance products and accelerate customer acquisition and revenue.
schemes.eligibility: Startups with a live tech product that generated Rs. 10 lakhs revenue in last 6 months OR raised at least Rs. 30 lakhs external equity; 50% margin money; valid KSUM Unique ID.

schemes.name: Seed Fund (Seed Support Scheme)
schemes.benefit: Soft loan up to Rs. 15 lakhs at 6% simple interest per annum
schemes.description: Soft loan to early-stage startups for product development, customer development, marketing and scaling up, disbursed in tranches with a 12-month moratorium.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered in Kerala as LLP/Pvt Ltd with active KSUM Unique ID and DPIIT recognition, working on an innovative product (not service/trade).

schemes.name: Patent (IPR) Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 2 lakhs per Indian patent; up to Rs. 10 lakhs per international patent
schemes.description: Reimburses government filing charges and IP attorney/consultant fees for patent filing (excludes renewal and appellate costs).
schemes.eligibility: Kerala-based startups registered with DPIIT and KSUM, less than 10 years old, patents filed on or after 9 November 2015 and not reimbursed elsewhere.

schemes.name: Early Stage Equity / Fund of Funds
schemes.benefit: Ticket size Rs. 25 lakhs to Rs. 200 lakhs
schemes.description: KSUM partners with SEBI-registered venture capital funds to invest early-stage risk capital in emerging technology startups.
schemes.eligibility: Early-stage technology-based startups registered in Kerala.

schemes.name: Government as Marketplace
schemes.benefit: Direct procurement up to Rs. 20 lakhs; limited-tender purchases up to Rs. 100 lakhs
schemes.description: Enables government departments to procure innovative products and services directly from registered startups, giving them their first public customers.
schemes.eligibility: KSUM-registered startups with an innovative product or service.

schemes.name: Marketing Support
schemes.benefit: 50% subsidy on marketing expenses
schemes.description: Subsidises marketing spend including digital and print media and PR activities to help startups reach markets.
schemes.eligibility: KSUM-registered startups.

schemes.name: Rent (Lease Rental) Subsidy
schemes.benefit: Reimbursement of a portion of rent paid, for a maximum of 3 years
schemes.description: Reimburses part of the rent paid for built-up space in government parks or government-owned/leased premises to support scale-up-stage startups.
schemes.eligibility: Startups showing at least 50% year-on-year revenue growth, with annual revenue of at least Rs. 50 lakhs or external equity funding of at least Rs. 150 lakhs.

schemes.name: Incubation & IEDC Support
schemes.benefit: 24/7 workspace, fab labs, business services and mentorship
schemes.description: Provides incubation infrastructure, prototyping/fabrication facilities and mentoring through KSUM incubators and campus Innovation & Entrepreneurship Development Centres (IEDCs).
schemes.eligibility: Startups and student entrepreneurs in Kerala.

states.nodalAgency: Department of Higher Education, UT Administration of Ladakh (implemented by Entrepreneurship Development Institute of India, EDII)
states.summary: Ladakh has no distinct financial startup scheme of its own yet; entrepreneurship support is delivered through the 'Enterprising Ladakh' programme (EDII with the UT Administration), which provides training, mentoring and incubation support and is developing a formal Startup, Innovation and Entrepreneurship Policy.

schemes.name: Enterprising Ladakh (EDIL) support
schemes.benefit: Training, mentoring and incubation support (no fixed grant amount published)
schemes.description: Flagship programme establishing the Entrepreneurship Development Institute of Ladakh, offering entrepreneurship training, a pool of startup mentors, and handholding for new ventures.
schemes.eligibility: Aspiring and existing entrepreneurs/startups in Ladakh

states.nodalAgency: Department of Industries, UT Administration of Lakshadweep
states.summary: Lakshadweep has no distinct startup policy; entrepreneurship support is provided mainly through a general 25% Capital Investment Subsidy Scheme for micro and small enterprises, with startups also relying on central schemes (e.g. SISFS, PMEGP).

schemes.name: 25% Capital Investment Subsidy Scheme
schemes.benefit: 25% capital investment subsidy
schemes.description: Subsidy on capital investment for micro and small entrepreneurial units in the UT, per revised guidelines issued by the administration.
schemes.eligibility: Micro and small entrepreneurial units in Lakshadweep

states.policy: Madhya Pradesh Startup Policy and Implementation Scheme 2022
states.nodalAgency: Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME); MP Startup Centre under Madhya Pradesh Laghu Udyog Nigam (MPLUN)
states.summary: Madhya Pradesh's 2022 startup policy supports DPIIT-recognised, MP-registered startups with a strong focus on product-based and agri/food ventures, offering investment assistance, lease-rental and patent reimbursement, employment and training support, incubation grants, procurement preference and a State Innovation Challenge grant of up to Rs.1 crore.
sectors: Innovation and technology solving economic-social problems

schemes.name: Assistance on Investment Received
schemes.benefit: 15% of investment, up to Rs.15 lakh per investment (max 4 stages); additional 20% for women-led startups
schemes.description: Startups that raise funds from a SEBI/RBI-recognised financial institution get 15% of the investment reimbursed, up to Rs.15 lakh, payable separately for up to four investment stages over the startup's life.
schemes.eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups registered in MP that have received recognised investment; extra 20% for startups with 51%+ women stakeholding

schemes.name: Lease Rental Assistance
schemes.benefit: 50% of monthly rent, up to Rs.5,000/month for 3 years
schemes.description: Reimbursement of 50% of the rent paid on workspace leased by startups (also available on lease rent and maintenance charges in state-owned/aided industrial areas for product-based startups), capped at Rs.5,000 per month for up to three years.
schemes.eligibility: Startups established/registered in Madhya Pradesh leasing workspace

schemes.name: Patent Assistance
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs.5 lakh per patent
schemes.description: Reimbursement of up to Rs.5 lakh for obtaining a patent, provided the patent is obtained for a startup established in the state.
schemes.eligibility: Startups established in Madhya Pradesh obtaining a patent

schemes.name: Employment Generation Assistance
schemes.benefit: Rs.5,000 per employee per month, up to 25 employees for up to 3 years
schemes.description: Product-based startup units get Rs.5,000 per new employee per month for the first three years from commencement of commercial production, subject to minimum local-employment thresholds (50%/75%/90% MP natives by year 1/2/3).
schemes.eligibility: Product-based startup units; new employees appointed within first 3 years of commercial production; max 25 employees

schemes.name: Training Expenditure Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Rs.13,000 per employee per year for 3 years, up to 25 employees
schemes.description: Reimbursement of skill development and training expenditure for startup employees at Rs.13,000 per employee per year for three years.
schemes.eligibility: Startups employing workers domiciled in Madhya Pradesh; max 25 employees

schemes.name: State Innovation Challenge Grant
schemes.benefit: Grant up to Rs.1 crore (in up to 4 phases) plus procurement and licence-fee exemptions
schemes.description: Special financial assistance for concepts selected to solve four high-impact economic-social problems of the state, including a grant up to Rs.1 crore, exemption/reimbursement of licence and consent charges, and two years of procurement support.
schemes.eligibility: Startups and institutions whose concepts are selected by the state-level Empowered Committee under the State Innovation Challenge

schemes.name: Concession in Electricity Tariff and Electricity Duty Exemption
schemes.benefit: Electricity at fixed Rs.5/unit for 3 years; full electricity duty exemption for 3 years
schemes.description: Product-based startup units on new electricity connections get supply at a fixed Rs.5 per unit for three years and full exemption from electricity duty for three years from the date of connection.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible new product-based startup units with new electricity connections

schemes.name: Incubation Upgradation Assistance
schemes.benefit: One-time Rs.5 lakh
schemes.description: One-time assistance of Rs.5 lakh to upgrade an incubator, available once in the incubator's life, conditional on increasing existing seat capacity by an additional 20%.
schemes.eligibility: Incubators located in Madhya Pradesh with Startup India recognition

schemes.name: Event Organizing Assistance for Incubators
schemes.benefit: Rs.5 lakh per event, up to Rs.20 lakh per annum
schemes.description: Assistance of Rs.5 lakh per event to incubators located in Madhya Pradesh for organising startup-related events, capped at Rs.20 lakh per annum.
schemes.eligibility: Incubators located in Madhya Pradesh

schemes.name: Marketing and Procurement Preference
schemes.benefit: Exemption from experience/turnover conditions in government tenders up to Rs.1 crore; EMD/Security deposit exemption
schemes.description: Under the MP Store Purchase and Service Procurement Rules, startups get exemption from prior-experience and turnover requirements for government tenders up to Rs.1 crore, acceptance of Proof of Concept, and exemption from EMD/security deposit on state tenders.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered in Madhya Pradesh participating in government procurement

schemes.name: Margin Money and Interest Subsidy (via MSME Development Policy)
schemes.benefit: Margin money up to 15% (max Rs.12 lakh); interest subsidy up to 5% (6% for SC/ST/OBC/women), up to Rs.5 lakh/year for 7 years
schemes.description: Startups can access the prevailing MSME Development Policy benefits including margin money assistance and interest subsidy on term loans, with enhanced rates for SC/ST/OBC and women entrepreneurs.
schemes.eligibility: DPIIT-recognised MP startups eligible under the prevailing MSME Development Policy

states.policy: Maharashtra Startup, Entrepreneurship and Innovation Policy 2025
states.nodalAgency: Maharashtra State Innovation Society (MSInS), Dept. of Skills, Employment, Entrepreneurship & Innovation
states.summary: Maharashtra runs its startup ecosystem through the Maharashtra State Innovation Society (MSInS), whose 2025 policy targets 50,000 recognised startups and 1.25 lakh entrepreneurs over five years, backed by a Rs 500 crore Maha-Fund plus fiscal incentives (patent/IPR, SGST, stamp-duty, testing and marketing reimbursements) and government procurement pilots.

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Maha-Fund
schemes.benefit: Rs 500 crore corpus; Rs 5-10 lakh support per early-stage entrepreneur
schemes.description: Flagship state fund providing early-stage funding, mentoring and incubation through a three-stage selection, aiming to back 25,000 entrepreneurs from a pool of 5 lakh youth.
schemes.eligibility: Early-stage entrepreneurs, with priority to youth, women and rural founders

schemes.name: Startup Week Government Work Orders
schemes.benefit: Pilot work orders up to Rs 25 lakh
schemes.description: Startups selected at the annual Startup Week pitch solutions to government departments and win pilot procurement work orders for implementation.
schemes.eligibility: Startups selected through MSInS Startup Week

schemes.name: Departmental Innovation Fund
schemes.benefit: 0.5% of each state department's annual budget earmarked for innovation
schemes.description: Every state government department must set aside 0.5% of its annual outlay to promote innovation and entrepreneurship, channelled through MSInS.
schemes.eligibility: Routed via MSInS to startups and innovation programmes

schemes.name: Patent / IPR Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 80% of patent filing cost, up to Rs 2 lakh (Indian patent) / Rs 10 lakh (international patent)
schemes.description: Reimburses a share of patent filing costs to encourage startups to protect their intellectual property.
schemes.eligibility: MSInS/DPIIT-recognised startups registered in Maharashtra

schemes.name: SGST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Reimbursement of State GST paid
schemes.description: Startups can claim back the State GST paid where input tax credit is not available, easing early-stage cash flow.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible registered startups under the Maharashtra startup policy

schemes.name: Stamp Duty & Registration Fee Exemption
schemes.benefit: 100% exemption for first 3 years, 50% for the next 3 years
schemes.description: Exemption from stamp duty and registration charges on property/office lease agreements for recognised startups.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Maharashtra startups

schemes.name: Quality Testing & Certification Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 80% of testing cost at BIS-accredited facilities
schemes.description: Reimburses most of the cost of product quality testing and certification at accredited labs.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Maharashtra startups

schemes.name: Marketing / Exhibition Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Rs 2 lakh (domestic) / Rs 5 lakh (international) exhibition participation
schemes.description: Supports startups' participation in domestic and international trade shows and exhibitions to build markets.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Maharashtra startups

schemes.name: Incubation Support
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 5 crore per incubation centre
schemes.description: State grant support for new and existing incubators, plus micro-incubators in ITIs/polytechnics and regional innovation hubs across administrative divisions.
schemes.eligibility: Approved incubators and host institutions

states.nodalAgency: Planning Department, Government of Manipur (in collaboration with NEDFi)
states.summary: Manipur's startup programme, run by the state Planning Department, supports Manipur-domiciled entrepreneurs through stage-based seed grants and subsidised bank-loan packages plus IPR and stamp-duty reimbursements, with dedicated support for SC/ST/OBC/women/minority founders.

schemes.name: Idea Stage Seed Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 3 lakh (two installments of Rs. 1.5 lakh)
schemes.description: Seed grant for proof of concept, prototype development and market entry, with mandatory business incubation training/boot camp and mentorship.
schemes.eligibility: Manipur domicile by birth; individual/group with an innovative, scalable business idea

schemes.name: Revenue Stage Support
schemes.benefit: 30% subsidy up to Rs. 30 lakh (65% bank loan, 5% margin money)
schemes.description: Scale-up assistance for existing entrepreneurs expanding a proven business, structured as a subsidy on top of a bank-financed project.
schemes.eligibility: Manipur domicile; existing entrepreneurs seeking business expansion

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Entrepreneurship Support Scheme (CMESS)
schemes.benefit: Project support up to Rs. 10 lakh with 30% subsidy from Manipur Startup Fund (65% bank loan, 5% margin money)
schemes.description: Support scheme for unemployed youth to set up bankable enterprises, with a 30% government subsidy on financially viable project cost.
schemes.eligibility: Manipur domicile, unemployed, age 18-65; one applicant per family; business not older than 2 years; no prior startup subsidy

schemes.name: ESS / Micro Entrepreneur Stage
schemes.benefit: Rs. 1 lakh subsidy plus Rs. 2 lakh bank loan
schemes.description: Support package for existing micro entrepreneurs.
schemes.eligibility: Manipur domicile; existing micro entrepreneurs

schemes.name: Stand Up Stage
schemes.benefit: Same as Revenue stage (30% subsidy up to Rs. 30 lakh) for new projects
schemes.description: Dedicated support for deprived sections to start new projects on the same terms as the Revenue stage.
schemes.eligibility: SC / ST / OBC / Women / Minority applicants with new projects

schemes.name: Patent / IPR Filing Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% of actual costs up to Rs. 2 lakh (domestic) / Rs. 5 lakh (international); 75% on filing, 25% on grant
schemes.description: Reimbursement of patent filing, attorney, search and maintenance fees for recognised startups.
schemes.eligibility: Registered/recognised Manipur startups filing patents

schemes.name: Stamp Duty / Registration Fee Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% reimbursement of stamp duty / registration fee / conversion fee on first transaction
schemes.description: Full reimbursement of stamp duty, registration and conversion fees paid on sale/lease deeds for the startup's first transaction.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Manipur startups (first transaction)

schemes.name: Manipur Startup Venture Fund (MSVF)
schemes.description: Collaborative venture fund between the Government of Manipur and NEDFi to provide risk capital to startups.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Manipur startups

states.nodalAgency: PRIME Meghalaya (Promotion and Incubation of Market-driven Enterprises), Government of Meghalaya; Department of Planning & Finance
states.summary: Meghalaya supports startups mainly through PRIME (Promotion and Incubation of Market-driven Enterprises), launched in 2019, which offers stage-wise grants and interest-free loans, incubation and mentoring via PRIME Hubs, plus policy-level incentives such as GST, stamp duty and power reimbursements.

schemes.name: PRIME Kick Start Grant
schemes.benefit: Non-returnable grant up to Rs 10 lakh
schemes.description: Early-stage grant to support research and development and help innovators transform concepts into viable, market-ready solutions.
schemes.eligibility: Early-stage startups from Meghalaya working on an innovative/unique product or service

schemes.name: PRIME Scale-up Innovation Loan
schemes.benefit: Zero-interest, collateral-free soft loan up to Rs 75 lakh
schemes.description: Interest-free loan for startups with existing revenue to scale operations, refine business models and expand market reach.
schemes.eligibility: Startup entrepreneurs / community-led enterprises with an existing innovative business needing capital to scale

schemes.name: PRIME Small Support Grant
schemes.benefit: Grant up to Rs 3 lakh
