schemes.description: Financial assistance for infrastructure upgrades and technology adoption to improve productivity of small enterprises.
schemes.eligibility: Registered micro or small enterprises

schemes.name: PRIME InnoVenture Grant
schemes.benefit: Grant up to Rs 35 lakh
schemes.description: Funds transformative ideas that address key challenges and have potential for sector-wide or cross-sectoral impact.
schemes.eligibility: High-impact innovators / startups at scaling-up stage

schemes.name: GST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: State GST reimbursed for 7 years, up to Rs 7 lakh per startup per year
schemes.description: Annual reimbursement of state GST paid on sale of goods for eligible startups.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups under the Meghalaya Startup Policy

schemes.name: Power Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 50% subsidy on power consumption, up to Rs 10 lakh per year for 5 years
schemes.description: Subsidy on power charges to reduce operating costs for eligible startups.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups under the Meghalaya Startup Policy

schemes.name: Patent Filing Cost Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% of filing cost up to Rs 2 lakh (domestic) or Rs 5 lakh (international)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of patent filing costs to support protection of startup intellectual property.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups under the Meghalaya Startup Policy

schemes.name: Stamp Duty & Lease Rental Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% stamp duty on first transaction; 50% lease rental for 3 years up to Rs 2 lakh/year
schemes.description: Reimbursement of stamp duty/registration/conversion fees on first sale or lease deed, plus partial lease rental support for startups.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups under the Meghalaya Startup Policy

states.nodalAgency: Mizoram Startup Mission (MSUM), Planning & Programme Implementation Department / Entrepreneurship Development Centre (EDC)
states.summary: Mizoram supports startups through the Mizoram Entrepreneurship & Startup Policy, 2019, implemented by the Mizoram Startup Mission and Entrepreneurship Development Centre, offering micro seed grants, research funding, awareness support, acceleration and a state-backed venture capital fund. It targets young indigenous Mizo entrepreneurs, aiming to build self-employment and reduce dependence on public-sector jobs.

schemes.name: Mizoram Rahbi (Micro Startup Capital / Seed Grant Competition)
schemes.benefit: Grant not exceeding Rs. 5 lakh or half the estimate of the business plan, whichever is lower
schemes.description: A micro seed-capital competition ('Rahbi' means stepping stone) for early-stage entrepreneurs yet to gain traction; winners must match the balance of the business-plan cost through own funds or a bank loan.
schemes.eligibility: Residents of Mizoram, particularly young indigenous early-stage entrepreneurs

schemes.name: Bana Kaih Venture Capital Fund (Senhri Investment Fund)
schemes.benefit: Target corpus of Rs. 100 crore (Government contribution of Rs. 20 crore, balance mobilised from other sources)
schemes.description: A SEBI-registered pooled venture capital fund providing investment (not grants) to Mizoram entrepreneurs with scalable, innovative and technology-driven business models for startup initiation and expansion.
schemes.eligibility: Mizoram entrepreneurs with high-growth, scalable and innovative ventures

schemes.name: Research Funding
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 10 lakh per sector research project
schemes.description: Funding support for research projects on priority sectors under the Mizoram Entrepreneurship & Startup Policy.
schemes.eligibility: As per policy guidelines

schemes.name: Acceleration Programme
schemes.benefit: Seed investment plus mentorship and logistical support
schemes.description: An acceleration programme combining seed investment with mentorship and logistical support to help startups scale.
schemes.eligibility: As per policy guidelines

schemes.name: Mizoram Outstanding Entrepreneurs Award
schemes.benefit: Cash award of Rs. 50,000 per awardee, plus citation and trophy
schemes.description: Recognition award for outstanding entrepreneurs in the state to encourage entrepreneurship.
schemes.eligibility: Entrepreneurs in Mizoram

schemes.name: Entrepreneurship Awareness Programmes
schemes.benefit: Grant up to Rs. 25,000 per event (minimum 300 attendees)
schemes.description: Financial support for organising entrepreneurship awareness events to build a startup culture across the state.
schemes.eligibility: Event organisers meeting minimum attendance criteria

schemes.name: Startup Hub and Incubation Centre, Aizawl
schemes.benefit: Incubation space and support for startups
schemes.description: A state startup hub and incubation centre in Aizawl providing incubation, mentorship and ecosystem support to Mizoram startups.
schemes.eligibility: Mizoram-based startups and entrepreneurs

states.nodalAgency: Department of Industries & Commerce, Government of Nagaland (implemented via the Nagaland Innovation Society)
states.summary: Nagaland's Start-Up Policy 2019 aims to make the state a regional startup leader by nurturing local, rural, social and women-led enterprises through a mix of grants, subsidies and reimbursements administered by the Department of Industries & Commerce. Eligibility requires registration in Nagaland with an indigenous founder and at least 60% local workforce.

schemes.name: Seed Grant
schemes.benefit: One-time seed grant up to INR 1.00 lakh at the ideation stage
schemes.description: A one-time ideation-stage grant awarded to startups depending on the creativity and innovation of the idea.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible startups registered under the policy at ideation stage

schemes.name: Innovation Catalyst Grant (ICG)
schemes.benefit: One-time grant up to INR 5.00 lakh
schemes.description: Awarded to startups with the most innovative, scalable product addressing a critical problem within the State.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups with high-impact, scalable products

schemes.name: GST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Annual reimbursement of State GST up to INR 1.50 lakh per startup per year for 3 years (women-led get additional 30% for 2 more years)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of State GST paid on sales of goods and services for the first three years of operation.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups submitting GSTIN and filed GST returns

schemes.name: Digital Up-gradation Subsidy
schemes.benefit: One-time subsidy at 50% of capital expenditure, ceiling INR 2.00 lakh
schemes.description: Supports purchase of computers, smartphones, related hardware/software and broadband connectivity.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups

schemes.name: Power Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 50% of actual power cost/bill, up to INR 1.00 lakh per year for 2 years
schemes.description: Subsidy on electricity bills to reduce operating costs of startups.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups

schemes.name: Trademark/Patent/IPR Filing Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to 100% of filing cost, max INR 1.00 lakh for domestic patent (INR 1.5 lakh for women-led)
schemes.description: Reimburses filing, attorney, search and maintenance fees; paid 50% on filing and 50% on grant of the patent.
schemes.eligibility: Startups selected under the policy

schemes.name: Marketing and Promotion Assistance
schemes.benefit: 50% of actual marketing and promotion costs, up to INR 2.00 lakh per startup
schemes.description: Covers online/offline advertising, in-store promotions, trade show participation, market research and related travel.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups

schemes.name: Company Registration & Compliance Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 50,000 in the 1st year plus 50% of compliance cost for 2 years up to INR 30,000
schemes.description: Reimburses incorporation costs under Company/Partnership/LLP Acts and DPIIT registration fees.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups incorporating as a legal entity

schemes.name: Women-Led Startup Milestone-Based Seed Funding
schemes.benefit: INR 1.50 lakh per women-led startup, up to a maximum of 10 startups per year
schemes.description: Seed funding for women-led startups disbursed against measurable milestones such as DPR, revenue forecasts, social impact and scalability.
schemes.eligibility: Women-led startups (>51% women shareholding, indigenous inhabitant)

schemes.name: Women Digital Marketing & E-commerce Support
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 4.00 lakh per women entrepreneur (INR 2.00 lakh digital up-gradation + INR 2.00 lakh marketing assistance)
schemes.description: Supports women startups migrating to digital marketing, advertising and e-commerce platforms, provided 90% of target consumers are women or products are women-focused.
schemes.eligibility: Women-led startups with women-focused products/services

schemes.name: Incubation Support
schemes.benefit: Capital grant up to INR 25.00 lakh per incubator; reimbursement up to INR 1.00 lakh per incubate on graduation; INR 30.00 lakh set aside for existing/new incubators
schemes.description: Funds setting up and equipping incubation centres and reimburses incubators for successfully graduating startups with a scalable POC; mentorship fees of INR 1.00 lakh per year also provided.
schemes.eligibility: Government-recognised incubators and higher/technical educational institutions

schemes.name: Public Procurement Preference
schemes.benefit: Waiver of prior experience and turnover requirements in State tenders
schemes.description: Encourages startup participation in public procurement and mandates eligible companies to partner startups for software and infrastructure projects.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups whose products meet required specifications

states.nodalAgency: Directorate of Industries / Startup Odisha, Department of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of Odisha
states.summary: Odisha's Startup Policy offers recognized startups a monthly sustenance allowance, product-development and marketing assistance, patent (IPR) and SGST reimbursement, subsidized incubation/lease rental, seed and incubator funding, and public-procurement relaxations, with enhanced benefits for women, transgender and SC/ST/SEBC/PH founders. The policy is broadly sector-agnostic.

schemes.name: Monthly Sustenance Allowance
schemes.benefit: Rs. 20,000/month for 1 year (Rs. 22,000/month if founder/co-founder is a Woman, transgender or from SC/ST/SEBC/PH)
schemes.description: Monthly allowance paid to a recognized startup entity to help founders sustain themselves during the early stage.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized Startup Odisha entity

schemes.name: Need-Based / Product Development Assistance
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 10 lakh
schemes.description: Assistance towards cost of raw material, components and related equipment required for developing a new innovative product.
schemes.eligibility: Certified/recognized startup

schemes.name: Marketing / Publicity Assistance
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 5 lakh (combined product-development + marketing up to Rs. 16 lakh where a Woman/transgender/SC/ST/SEBC/PH founder holds at least 50% equity)
schemes.description: Support for introducing the innovated product into the market.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startup post product development

schemes.name: Patent / IPR Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% reimbursement up to Rs. 10 lakh
schemes.description: Reimbursement of the cost of filing and prosecution of patent applications.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startup filing patents

schemes.name: SGST / VAT-CST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Reimbursement of VAT/CST (SGST) paid in Odisha; exemption from VAT scrutiny for 3 years from certification
schemes.description: State tax paid by the startup company in Odisha is reimbursed as per the prevailing Industrial Policy.
schemes.eligibility: Certified startup company

schemes.name: Incubation / Lease Rental Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 50% of monthly rental of incubation space, up to Rs. 5,000/month, for a maximum of 1 year
schemes.description: Rental subsidy for startups incubated at any incubator recognized by the Startup Odisha Initiative.
schemes.eligibility: Startup incubated at a recognized incubator

schemes.name: Public Procurement Support
schemes.benefit: 10% of public procurement reserved for startups (in addition to 20% for MSEs); EMD exemption; relaxation of prior turnover and prior experience criteria
schemes.description: Startups registered under Startup Odisha / Startup India get EMD exemption and relaxation from prior-turnover and prior-experience conditions in open government tenders.
schemes.eligibility: Startup Odisha / Startup India registered startups

schemes.name: Seed Fund (Startup Odisha Seed Fund)
schemes.benefit: Government participates as Limited Partner up to 25% of fund size; initial corpus of Rs. 100 crore
schemes.description: State contributes as a limited partner alongside private investors to provide seed/early-stage capital to startups.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups via participating funds

schemes.name: Incubator Support Grants
schemes.benefit: One-time capital grant of 50% of capital cost (excl. building) up to Rs. 1 crore; performance grant of Rs. 5 lakh per successfully incubated startup; matching grant up to Rs. 5 crore for universities
schemes.description: Financial support to set up and operate incubators recognized under the Startup Odisha Initiative.
schemes.eligibility: Institutions / incubators recognized by Startup Odisha

states.policy: Aspiring Puducherry - Innovation and Startup Policy 2019
states.nodalAgency: Department of Industries and Commerce, Government of Puducherry (Puducherry Startup Cell)
states.summary: Puducherry's Innovation and Startup Policy 2019 sets up a Puducherry Startup Cell and a Rs 10 crore Puducherry Startup Fund to build incubators and co-working spaces, and offers startups seed grants, monthly sustenance allowances, marketing and infrastructure support, along with fiscal incentives carried over from the Industrial Policy 2016.

schemes.name: Startup Grant (Seed Grant)
schemes.benefit: Financial grant up to Rs 3 lakhs
schemes.description: Financial grant of up to Rs 3 lakhs provided to projects recommended by incubators established by the Government/Private Sector/Academic Institutions, approved and monitored by the Committee of experts under the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Committee (IEDC).
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups whose projects are recommended by an incubator

schemes.name: Sustenance Allowance
schemes.benefit: Rs 10,000/month per person (max 5 persons); Rs 15,000/month if founder/co-founder is Women/SC/PH/Transgender
schemes.description: Monthly sustenance allowance of Rs 10,000 per person up to a maximum of 5 persons; enhanced to Rs 15,000 per month where the founder or co-founder is a Woman/SC/Physically Handicapped/Transgender person.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups, on recommendation of incubators

schemes.name: Puducherry Startup Fund
schemes.benefit: Rs 10 crore corpus fund
schemes.description: A corpus fund of Rs 10 crores to support establishment of startup incubators and co-working spaces, and to provide infrastructure support such as workstations, cloud services, laboratory, testing and marketing facilities to startups on the recommendation of incubators.
schemes.eligibility: Incubators, co-working spaces and startups recommended by incubators

schemes.name: Fiscal Incentives (Industrial Policy 2016)
schemes.benefit: All Industrial Policy 2016 incentives; combined Centre + UT incentives capped at 75% of the capex of a component
schemes.description: All incentives available under the Industrial Policy 2016 are extended to startups, and startups may claim State, Government of India and GoI-agency incentives, subject to a combined cap of 75% of the capex of that component.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups

schemes.name: Marketing Assistance
schemes.benefit: Marketing support at MVP stage
schemes.description: Marketing assistance provided by the Startup Cell with the approval of IEDC to recognized startups operating in Puducherry at the Minimum Viable Product (MVP) stage.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups at MVP stage in Puducherry

schemes.name: Incubation and Infrastructure Support (Non-Fiscal)
schemes.benefit: Free cloud server, ERP software, and free access to universities/government libraries and laboratories
schemes.description: Cloud server with necessary software connecting all incubators, free Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software, and free access to universities, government libraries and government laboratories through incubators, plus R&D/testing/fab-lab facilities and shared office space.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups through incubators

schemes.name: IPR Facilitation
schemes.benefit: Fast-tracking and advisory on patents, trademarks and designs
schemes.description: The Puducherry Startup Cell facilitates filing of patents, trademarks and designs, fast-tracks startup patent applications for examination and disposal, and provides general advisory on protecting and promoting IPRs.
schemes.eligibility: Innovative recognized startups

schemes.name: Self-Certification Compliance
schemes.benefit: Self-certification for 6 labour and 3 environment laws; no labour inspections for 3 years
schemes.description: Recognized startups tied to recognized incubators may self-certify compliance for six labour and three environment laws, with no labour inspections for a period of 3 years except on credible violation reports with prior approval of the Labour Commissioner.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups signed with recognized incubators/accelerators

schemes.name: Public Procurement Support
schemes.benefit: Exemption from prior experience, turnover and security deposit criteria
schemes.description: Norms for public entities to procure from startups, with exemptions in criteria such as prior experience, turnover and security deposits (no exemption in quality, technical specifications and standards).
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups bidding for public procurement

states.nodalAgency: Department of Industries & Commerce, Government of Punjab (implemented via Startup Punjab / Punjab Infotech)
states.summary: Punjab supports startups under its Industrial & Business Development Policy through the Startup Punjab programme, offering seed grants, interest and lease-rental subsidies, IPR and stamp-duty reimbursement, incubation support and a dedicated scale-up fund.

schemes.name: Seed Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 3 lakh per startup
schemes.description: One-time seed grant to support idea validation, prototyping, travel and market/field research or skill training.
schemes.eligibility: Startups recognised under the policy, disbursed through recognised incubators

schemes.name: Interest Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 8% per annum for 5 years, up to INR 5 lakh per year
schemes.description: Subsidy on interest paid on loans from banks/financial institutions to reduce cost of capital for early-stage startups.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups availing loans from banks/FIs

schemes.name: Lease Rental Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 25% of lease rental for 1 year, up to INR 3 lakh per year
schemes.description: Reimbursement of a share of office/workspace lease rentals to lower operating costs during the first year.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups leasing approved space

schemes.name: Stamp Duty Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% reimbursement on MoA/AoA registration
schemes.description: Full reimbursement of stamp duty paid on incorporation documents (Memorandum/Articles of Association).
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups incorporating in Punjab

schemes.name: Patent / IPR Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 2 lakh for domestic patents and up to INR 10 lakh for international patents
schemes.description: Reimbursement of costs incurred by startups in filing and prosecuting domestic and international patents.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups filing patents

schemes.name: Sustenance Allowance
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 20,000-25,000 per month for up to 12 months
schemes.description: Monthly allowance to help founders sustain themselves during the early stage, subject to eligibility and performance milestones.
schemes.eligibility: Registered manufacturing/eligible startups under the policy

schemes.name: Scale-up Funding / Punjab Innovation Fund
schemes.benefit: INR 100 crore corpus deployed via SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Fund
schemes.description: State-backed fund-of-funds providing venture capital participation to help startups scale.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups selected for VC/AIF investment

schemes.name: Incubator Support
schemes.benefit: Capital grant up to INR 1 crore (government) / INR 50 lakh (private); operational & mentoring assistance up to INR 3 lakh/year for 5 years
schemes.description: Financial support to set up and run incubators, including operational subsidy, mentoring grants and event support to build startup infrastructure.
schemes.eligibility: Government and private incubators recognised under the policy

schemes.name: Women & SC Entrepreneurs Special Incentive
schemes.benefit: 25% of seed funding reserved, plus additional interest subsidy
schemes.description: Reservation of a share of seed funding and enhanced financial incentives to encourage women and Scheduled Caste entrepreneurs.
schemes.eligibility: Women and SC-led recognised startups

schemes.name: Marketing Assistance
schemes.benefit: Financial support for participation in domestic and international marketing/trade events
schemes.description: Reimbursement of a share of costs for participating in domestic and international trade fairs, exhibitions and marketing activities.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups participating in approved marketing events

states.nodalAgency: Department of Information Technology & Communication (DoIT&C), Government of Rajasthan (iStart Rajasthan)
states.summary: Rajasthan supports startups end-to-end through its iStart platform under the Rajasthan Startup Policy 2022, offering stage-wise grants, sustenance support, marketing and incubation assistance, and its QRate startup rating system. Investment-linked incentives such as SGST reimbursement and interest subsidy are available to startups through the Rajasthan Investment Promotion Scheme (RIPS) 2022.

schemes.name: Sustenance Allowance
schemes.benefit: INR 10,000 per month for one year
schemes.description: Monthly sustenance support paid to innovators/founders at the idea or prototype stage to help them work full-time on their venture.
schemes.eligibility: Idea/prototype-stage startups registered on iStart and recommended by the Nodal Institution and approved by the Committee.

schemes.name: Pre-seed / Idea Viability Grant
schemes.benefit: INR 2,40,000 (additional INR 60,000 for women-led startups, i.e. up to INR 3,00,000)
schemes.description: One-time viability grant for pre-seed/idea-stage startups whose idea meets the Evaluation Committee criteria, with an enhanced amount for startups having more than 50% equity held by women founders.
schemes.eligibility: Pre-seed / idea-prototype stage startups registered under iStart; enhanced grant for startups with >50% women founder equity.

schemes.name: Marketing & Commercialization Assistance
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 10 lakh
schemes.description: Assistance to help startups launch and market their products or services, disbursed as grant, soft loan or equity.
schemes.eligibility: Pilot-stage startups, typically requiring a minimum of 25% funding from registered angel/venture funds or reputed incubators.

schemes.name: Seed Funding Support
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 60 lakh in the seed phase
schemes.description: Seed-stage financial assistance provided to eligible startups to help them scale after the idea/prototype phase.
schemes.eligibility: Seed-stage startups registered on iStart and approved through the funding committee process.

schemes.name: Loan and Equity Financial Assistance (scale-up)
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 2 crore as loan/financial assistance and up to INR 5 crore as equity
schemes.description: Larger-ticket financial support for growth-stage startups, provided as loans and as equity investment to help them scale.
schemes.eligibility: Growth/scale-up stage startups registered under iStart, subject to committee approval and QRate assessment.

schemes.name: Incubation Support & Host Institute Grant
schemes.benefit: Free incubation access for startups; one-time capital grant up to INR 50 lakh for host institutes/incubators
schemes.description: Free access to incubators, mentoring, labs and university facilities for startups, plus capital grants to institutions and private players (up to 25% of investment, capped at INR 50 lakh) to set up incubation centres.
schemes.eligibility: Registered startups (for free access); technology/management/R&D institutions, corporates and VCs (for setup grants).

schemes.name: QRate Startup Rating & Assessment
schemes.benefit: Startup rating (Bronze / Silver / Gold / Platinum / Signature) used to grade eligibility for differential incentives
schemes.description: A periodic startup rating and assessment mechanism that evaluates startups' potential and investment worthiness, with the rating used to determine access to financial assistance and incubation.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered on the iStart platform.

schemes.name: Government Procurement / Public Tender Access
schemes.benefit: Access to participate in Rajasthan government tenders (procurement relaxations for startups)
schemes.description: Registered iStart startups are enabled to participate in and supply to state government procurement/tenders.
schemes.eligibility: Startups registered and recognized under the iStart Rajasthan program.

schemes.name: SGST Reimbursement (via RIPS 2022)
schemes.benefit: Up to 75% reimbursement of net SGST for up to 7-10 years; women-led startups eligible for 100% SGST reimbursement for 2 years
schemes.description: Reimbursement of State GST deposited, available to eligible enterprises and startups under the Rajasthan Investment Promotion Scheme (RIPS) 2022, with an enhanced benefit for women-led startups.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible enterprises/startups making qualifying investment under RIPS-2022; enhanced women-led startup benefit for 2 years from registration.

schemes.name: Interest Subsidy (via RIPS 2022)
schemes.benefit: Up to 6% per annum interest subsidy on term loans, for up to 7 years
schemes.description: Interest subsidy on term loans availed by eligible enterprises/startups under the Rajasthan Investment Promotion Scheme (RIPS) 2022 to reduce cost of borrowing.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible enterprises/startups availing term loans under RIPS-2022.

states.nodalAgency: Department of Commerce & Industries, Government of Sikkim
states.summary: Sikkim's startup support is anchored by the Chief Minister's Startup Scheme (CMSS), which offers one-time non-refundable financial assistance to local youth entrepreneurs, plus a suite of fiscal incentives (GST, power, lease and patent reimbursements) administered by the Commerce & Industries Department.

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Startup Scheme (CMSS) - Financial Assistance
schemes.benefit: One-time non-refundable assistance of 25% of approved project cost (up to 35% for non-manufacturing projects ineligible for central subsidy); projects up to Rs. 20 lakh
schemes.description: Launched 15 August 2017 to help local unemployed youth set up new ventures; the state contributes a share of the promoter's project cost while banks finance the balance.
schemes.eligibility: Bonafide local youth with COI/Sikkim Subject Certificate, unemployed, class V pass, aged 15-40; one member per family

schemes.name: Sikkim Skilled Youth Startup Scheme
schemes.benefit: 50% subsidy on project cost for BPL applicants and 35% for others; bank loan up to Rs. 20 lakh
schemes.description: Employment-generation scheme launched 15 August 2020 providing subsidy-linked bank finance, with the applicant contributing 5%-15% of project cost.
schemes.eligibility: Local youth of Sikkim; higher subsidy for BPL category

schemes.name: State GST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Annual reimbursement of state GST paid on sale of goods for 3 years, up to Rs. 5 lakh per startup
schemes.description: Fiscal incentive reimbursing the state component of GST paid by eligible startups for their first three years.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups under the scheme

schemes.name: Power Subsidy
schemes.benefit: 50% subsidy on power for 5 years, up to Rs. 10 lakh annually
schemes.description: Reduces electricity costs for eligible startups during their initial operating years.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups under the scheme

schemes.name: Lease Rental Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 50% of lease rental costs for 3 years, maximum Rs. 5 lakh
schemes.description: Supports workspace costs for startups by reimbursing part of office/industrial rental.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups under the scheme

schemes.name: Patent Filing Cost Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 1 lakh for domestic patents and up to Rs. 5 lakh for international patents
schemes.description: Reimburses costs incurred by startups in filing and securing patents.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups under the scheme

schemes.name: Stamp Duty / Registration Fee Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% reimbursement of stamp duty / registration fees on first property transaction
schemes.description: Waives the cost of stamp duty and registration for a startup's first property transaction.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups under the scheme

schemes.name: Women, Transgender & Differently-Abled Hiring Incentive
schemes.benefit: One-time incentive of Rs. 5,000 per candidate, up to Rs. 1 lakh per startup, over 3 years
schemes.description: Encourages startups to employ women, transgender and differently-abled persons through a per-candidate hiring incentive.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups hiring eligible candidates

states.policy: Tamil Nadu Startup and Innovation Policy 2023
states.nodalAgency: StartupTN (Tamil Nadu Startup and Innovation Mission), a Section 8 company under the Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
states.summary: Tamil Nadu's revamped 2023 startup policy aims to make the state one of the top 20 global startup destinations by 2032 and to seed 15,000 startups, with a strong 'social justice' focus on inclusive entrepreneurship for women, SC/ST, transgender and differently-abled founders. It is delivered through StartupTN, which runs seed grants, equity funds, incubation and market-access programmes.
sectors: Emerging technologies (AI, ML, IoT, SaaS, deep tech)

schemes.name: Tamil Nadu Startup Seed Grant Fund (TANSEED)
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 15 lakh for green-tech, rural-impact and women-led startups; up to Rs. 10 lakh for other sectors (StartupTN takes ~3% equity)
schemes.description: Competitive seed grant awarded to early-stage startups selected through periodic cohorts to support product development, validation and market entry.
schemes.eligibility: Early-stage, StartupTN-recognised startups; priority to green-tech, rural-impact and women-led ventures

schemes.name: Co-Creation Fund
schemes.benefit: Rs. 100 crore
schemes.description: State fund that supports the creation and operation of select private-sector VC funds focused on regional startups, women-led ventures and climate action.
schemes.eligibility: Private VC funds and startups aligned to regional, women-led and climate-action priorities

schemes.name: Tamil Nadu SC/ST Startup Fund
schemes.benefit: Equity investment (reported up to Rs. 5 crore per eligible startup)
schemes.description: India's first dedicated SC/ST equity fund, investing in innovation-led, scalable startups founded by Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe entrepreneurs to address financial exclusion.
schemes.eligibility: Startups founded by SC/ST entrepreneurs

schemes.name: Grant-in-aid for Trans Persons and Differently-Abled Founders
schemes.benefit: Rs. 5 lakh grant-in-aid for 10 identified startups, plus priority access to TANSEED
schemes.description: Special grant supporting startups founded by transgender and differently-abled entrepreneurs as part of the policy's equitable-growth focus.
schemes.eligibility: Startups founded by transgender or differently-abled entrepreneurs

schemes.name: Social Justice Venture Lab
schemes.benefit: Tailored workshops, mentoring and post-investment support
schemes.description: A dedicated intervention programme providing hand-holding, guidance and support to entrepreneurs from marginalised communities.
schemes.eligibility: Entrepreneurs from SC/ST, transgender, differently-abled and other marginalised sections

schemes.name: Startup Thamizha (Shark Tank-style TV show)
schemes.benefit: Rs. 30-50 crore investment pool per season
schemes.description: A televised reality show in which startups pitch to investors on air for funding, raising visibility for the state ecosystem.
schemes.eligibility: Startups selected to pitch on the show

schemes.name: Smart Card Programme
schemes.benefit: Free or subsidised access to 50-60 service providers (legal, tech, marketing, financial)
schemes.description: A card giving budding entrepreneurs (Start STEPs) discounted or free access to a panel of startup service providers.
schemes.eligibility: Start STEP (budding, pre-registration) entrepreneurs

schemes.name: Patent (IPR) and Marketing Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Reimbursement of patent filing/prosecution fees and marketing / global-event participation costs
schemes.description: The policy provides reimbursements and incentives to help startups protect intellectual property and access domestic and global markets.
schemes.eligibility: StartupTN-recognised startups

schemes.name: Tamil Nadu Startup Fund of Funds
schemes.benefit: Rs. 250 crore corpus (SEBI-registered AIF, managed by SIDBI)
schemes.description: A fund-of-funds that channels capital into SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds, which in turn invest in Tamil Nadu-based startups.
schemes.eligibility: AIFs investing in state-based startups; startups accessed indirectly via these funds

states.nodalAgency: Department of Information Technology, Electronics & Communications (ITE&C), Government of Telangana (implemented via Telangana State Innovation Cell / TSIC, T-Hub and WE Hub)
states.summary: Telangana supports startups through the Telangana Innovation Policy 2016, offering tax and cost reimbursements plus incubation and seed-funding support delivered via TSIC, T-Hub and the women-focused WE Hub. Benefits are anchored around recognised startups incubated in state-supported incubators.

schemes.name: SGST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% SGST reimbursed for 3 years from date of incorporation, on a maximum turnover of INR 1 crore/annum
schemes.description: Recognised startups get their State GST reimbursed for the first three years of operation to reduce early-stage tax burden.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised Telangana startups; claims filed within 6 months after each half-year

schemes.name: Patent / IPR Registration Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 2 lakh per Indian patent and up to INR 10 lakh per foreign patent (50% on filing, 50% on grant)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of patent registration costs to encourage startups to protect their intellectual property.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups; available twice within the first 5 years

schemes.name: International Marketing Cost Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 30% of annual international marketing costs, capped at INR 5 lakh per company
schemes.description: Reimburses part of the cost of participating in global trade shows and marketing events (airfare, accommodation, fees).
schemes.eligibility: Startups endorsed by state-supported incubators; within first 3 years

schemes.name: Recruitment Assistance
schemes.benefit: INR 10,000 per employee in the first year of operations
schemes.description: One-time recruitment support per employee hired to offset early hiring costs, favouring local hiring.
schemes.eligibility: Employees must work minimum 8 months; at least 50% of staff of Telangana origin

schemes.name: Performance-Linked Grant
schemes.benefit: 5% of annual turnover, capped at INR 10 lakh
schemes.description: Growth-linked grant rewarding startups that demonstrate strong year-on-year revenue growth.
schemes.eligibility: Startups achieving 15% year-on-year growth within 3 years of incorporation (audited accounts required)

schemes.name: T-Seed Fund
schemes.benefit: Early-stage seed capital (corpus of INR 250 crore)
schemes.description: Seed fund to support innovators and early-stage founders with costs such as company registration, patent filing, certifications and travel; disbursed via T-Hub or partner incubators.
schemes.eligibility: Early-stage innovators and startups incubated at T-Hub / partner incubators

schemes.name: Telangana Innovation Fund (T-Fund)
schemes.benefit: Master fund (initial target INR 2000 crore) investing in sector-specific and general VC funds
schemes.description: A fund-of-funds set up with global investors and T-Hub to channel venture capital into Telangana startups.
schemes.eligibility: Startups backed through partner venture capital funds

schemes.name: T-Hub Incubation
schemes.benefit: Incubation, mentorship, co-working space, investor and corporate access
schemes.description: State-anchored incubator providing physical space, mentorship and connections to investors and corporates for startups across sectors.
schemes.eligibility: Startups selected into T-Hub programs

schemes.name: WE Hub (Women Entrepreneurs)
schemes.benefit: Access to capital, mentorship, infrastructure and support services for women-led startups
schemes.description: India's first state-led incubator dedicated to women entrepreneurs, offering comprehensive incubation, funding access and branding support.
schemes.eligibility: Women-led / women-founded startups and aspiring women entrepreneurs

states.nodalAgency: Directorate of Information Technology, Government of Tripura
states.summary: Tripura's IT Start-up Scheme 2019 supports IT and service-based startups recognised by the state with a package of grants, subsidies and reimbursements delivered through the Directorate of Information Technology and the Startup Tripura portal.

schemes.name: Seed Fund Grant
schemes.benefit: One-time grant of Rs. 2 lakh
schemes.description: All recognised startups are eligible for a one-time seed grant of Rs. 2 lakh after receiving the Recognition Certificate.
schemes.eligibility: State-recognised startups with Recognition Certificate

schemes.name: Patent (IPR) Filing Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs. 2 lakh per Indian patent; up to Rs. 10 lakh for foreign patents
schemes.description: Cost of filing and prosecuting patent applications is reimbursed to incubated startups, paid 50% after filing and 50% after the patent is granted.
schemes.eligibility: Incubated startups filing patents

schemes.name: Incubation / Lease-Rental Subsidy
schemes.benefit: Free incubation space for the first 3 years
schemes.description: Startups using the government incubation facility pay no rent for the first three years, with an optional fourth year on payment basis subject to State Level Implementation Committee approval.
schemes.eligibility: Startups requiring incubation facility

schemes.name: Bandwidth Cost Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% of bandwidth cost, up to Rs. 40,000 per year
schemes.description: Startups receive 100% reimbursement of internet bandwidth charges until the start of commercial production, capped at Rs. 40,000 per year.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups before commercial production

schemes.name: Power Charges Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 100% of power charges for up to 3 years
schemes.description: Startups are reimbursed 100% of actual power charges paid to TSECL until the start of commercial production or for three years, whichever is earlier.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups before commercial production

schemes.name: Capital Investment Subsidy
schemes.benefit: Up to 80% of project cost, maximum Rs. 10 lakh
schemes.description: Startups can receive a capital investment subsidy of up to 80% of project cost subject to a ceiling of Rs. 10 lakh.
schemes.eligibility: State-recognised startups

schemes.name: Recruitment / Employment Assistance
schemes.benefit: Rs. 10,000 per employee (one-time, first year)
schemes.description: A one-time recruitment assistance of Rs. 10,000 per employee is provided in the first year to support hiring.
schemes.eligibility: State-recognised startups

schemes.name: SGST Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Annual SGST reimbursement for first 3 years
schemes.description: Startups receive annual reimbursement of State GST for the first three years, subject to a maximum turnover of Rs. 1 crore.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups with turnover up to Rs. 1 crore

states.nodalAgency: Department of IT & Electronics, Government of Uttar Pradesh
states.summary: Uttar Pradesh supports startups through its StartinUP programme under the Startup Policy 2020, offering monthly sustenance allowance, prototype and seed/marketing grants, patent reimbursement, event support, incubator and Centre of Excellence grants, and a SIDBI-managed fund of funds, with enhanced benefits for women, Divyangjan, EWS and Purvanchal/Bundelkhand founders.

schemes.name: Sustenance Allowance
schemes.benefit: INR 17,500 per month per startup for one year
schemes.description: Monthly sustenance support to idea-stage startups to cover early operating needs, given to up to 25 startups per incubator per year.
schemes.eligibility: Idea-stage startups incubated at a recognised incubator; women/Transgender/Divyangjan cofounders (26%+ equity), EWS founders and Purvanchal/Bundelkhand startups get an additional 50%.

schemes.name: Prototype Development Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 5 Lakhs
schemes.description: Grant to help startups build and refine their minimum viable product (MVP).
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups developing a prototype/MVP.

schemes.name: Seed Capital / Marketing Assistance
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 7.5 Lakhs
schemes.description: Seed capital and marketing support to help startups launch their MVP in the market.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups launching an MVP; women/Divyangjan/Transgender/EWS and Purvanchal/Bundelkhand founders get an additional 50%.

schemes.name: Patent (IPR) Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 2 Lakhs (Indian patent); up to INR 10 Lakhs (International patent)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of costs for successfully filing domestic and international patents.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups filing successful patents.

schemes.name: Event Participation Support
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 50,000 (national); up to INR 1 Lakh (international)
schemes.description: Reimbursement to startups for participating in national and international events.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised startups attending eligible events.

schemes.name: Incubator Establishment Grant
schemes.benefit: Up to 50% of setup cost, max INR 1 crore (INR 1.25 crore in Purvanchal/Bundelkhand)
schemes.description: Capital grant to establish new incubators, with the first installment capped at 25%.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible host institutions setting up recognised incubators.

schemes.name: Incubator Operational Support
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 30 Lakhs per year for 5 years
schemes.description: Annual operational assistance to incubators hosting 10 or more startups.
schemes.eligibility: Recognised incubators with 10+ incubated startups.

schemes.name: Centre of Excellence (CoE) Grant-in-Aid
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 10 crores over 5 years
schemes.description: Capital and operational grant to establish sector-focused Centres of Excellence in emerging technologies.
schemes.eligibility: Institutions setting up a Centre of Excellence, contingent on move toward self-sustainability.

schemes.name: UP Startup Fund (Fund of Funds)
schemes.benefit: INR 1,000 crore corpus
schemes.description: A SIDBI-managed fund of funds that invests in daughter AIFs, which in turn invest in UP startups; it does not invest directly into startups.
schemes.eligibility: Startups funded via SIDBI-empanelled daughter funds/AIFs.

states.nodalAgency: Directorate of Industries, Department of MSME, Government of Uttarakhand (State Nodal Agency)
states.summary: Uttarakhand's 2023 startup policy offers ideation-stage sustenance allowances, one-time seed funding, IPR and incubation support, pilot-project and event grants, procurement relaxations and a state Funds-of-Funds, with enhanced benefits for women, SC/ST, grassroot/rural, differently-abled and disruptive-sector startups.
sectors: Disruptive sectors (futuristic technologies, renewable energy, climate change, circular economy, sustainability)

schemes.name: Sustenance / Monthly Allowance
schemes.benefit: Rs. 15,000/month (general & student startups); Rs. 20,000/month for women / grassroot or rural-impact / differently-abled / transgender startups, for up to 12 months
schemes.description: Monthly allowance for founders of ideation-stage startups working on Proof of Concept (PoC), given for a maximum of 12 months.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups at ideation stage working on PoC

schemes.name: One-time Seed Funding
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 10 lakhs (general); up to INR 12.5 lakhs for women/SC/ST/grassroot startups; additional INR 2.5 lakhs for disruptive startups
schemes.description: One-time seed grant for startups at prototyping, MVP or ideation stage to build and trial products/services.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups at prototyping/MVP/ideation stage

schemes.name: Need-Based Assistance
schemes.benefit: Up to INR 5 lakhs
schemes.description: Case-by-case assistance for raw materials, components and equipment required for product development.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups (need-based approval)

schemes.name: Intellectual Property (Patent/Trademark/Design) Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Patents: actual filing cost up to Rs. 1 lakh (Indian) and up to Rs. 5 lakhs (international), for up to two patents; Trademark: up to Rs. 10,000 for 2 TMs; Industrial Design: up to Rs. 10,000 for 2 IDs
schemes.description: Reimbursement of IP filing costs to encourage startups to create patents, trademarks and industrial designs.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups

schemes.name: Pre-Incubation Support
schemes.benefit: 4 months pre-incubation; cost up to INR 10,000 per startup borne by State Govt.
schemes.description: Pre-incubation mentoring and handholding at a state-recognized incubation centre for aspiring entrepreneurs, students, women and grassroot innovators.
schemes.eligibility: Aspiring entrepreneurs, student/women/grassroot startups

schemes.name: Free Incubation Support
schemes.benefit: One-time free-of-cost incubation for up to 12 months (additional 6 months for disruptive startups)
schemes.description: All recognized startups get free incubation support at any state government recognized incubation centre.
schemes.eligibility: All recognized startups

schemes.name: Pilot Project Assistance
schemes.benefit: One-time assistance up to INR 3 lakhs (additional INR 2.5 lakhs for disruptive startups)
schemes.description: Support for running a pilot project with a department/agency/body/authority of the Government of Uttarakhand (sandbox for startups).
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups undertaking a pilot with a government body

schemes.name: Market Access / Event Participation Assistance
schemes.benefit: Financial assistance to participate in two national and one international trade fair, conference, training or acceleration program
schemes.description: Support for eligible startups to gain national and international market exposure.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible recognized startups

schemes.name: Land Allocation in Industrial Estates
schemes.benefit: Allocation of land/plots at base (subsidized) price in notified Industrial Areas, IT parks, SEZs or Govt.-sponsored Industrial Estates
schemes.description: Enables recognized startups to obtain workspace at subsidized rates once they move out of an incubation centre.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups

schemes.name: Public Procurement Relaxations
schemes.benefit: Purchase up to INR 10 lakhs through limited tender or without tender (subject to Technical Committee approval); relaxation of prior turnover, prior experience and Earnest Money Deposit
schemes.description: Procurement preference and exemptions across state departments to help recognized startups participate in government tenders.
schemes.eligibility: Recognized startups

schemes.name: Funds of Funds (Uttarakhand Venture Fund)
schemes.benefit: Corpus fund of INR 200 crore for investing in venture funds that invest in startups
schemes.description: A state corpus, managed through a separate SPV, that invests in venture funds backing Uttarakhand startups; state agencies may also set up sector-specific venture funds.
schemes.eligibility: Startups via participating venture funds

schemes.name: Incubator Capital & Operational Grant
schemes.benefit: Capital grant up to INR 1 crore (new) / up to INR 50 lakhs (expansion); operational cost up to Rs. 1 lakh per startup plus additional up to INR 5 lakhs (Startup Council approval); support up to 5 years
schemes.description: Grants to establish or scale up PPP and academic incubators (excluding land and building cost), with tiered capital support for universities, HEIs and colleges.
schemes.eligibility: State-recognized incubators (PPP, academic, government)

states.nodalAgency: Directorate of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME), Department of MSME & Textiles, Government of West Bengal (State Start-up Council chaired by the Chief Secretary)
states.summary: West Bengal's Startup Policy 2016-2021 (run through the Directorate of MSME and the startupbengal.in portal) supports startups via early-stage/seed and sustenance funding, patent reimbursement, incubation and Entrepreneurship Development Centre grants, priority venture-capital access and land allocation. In its 2026 state budget the government announced a new startup policy along with a Rs 40 crore Incubation Fund and a Rs 60 crore Venture Capital Fund.
sectors: Social enterprises addressing rural service gaps

schemes.name: Seed / Kick-starter Funding
schemes.benefit: Early-stage seed funding reported at Rs 5-25 lakh for priority-sector startups
schemes.description: A network of kick-starter funds provides early-stage/seed financial support to innovative startups approved by the Screening Committee.
schemes.eligibility: Registered startups with innovative, scalable ideas in priority sectors

schemes.name: Monthly Sustenance Allowance
schemes.benefit: Rs 10,000 to Rs 25,000 per month
schemes.description: Monthly financial support to early-stage startups to cover founder sustenance during the development phase.
schemes.eligibility: Early-stage startups registered in West Bengal

schemes.name: Patent (IPR) Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Reimbursement of patent filing and prosecution costs (as per WB MSME Policy)
schemes.description: The cost of filing and prosecution of a patent application is reimbursed to incubated startup companies as per the MSME Policy of the Government of West Bengal.
schemes.eligibility: Incubated startups with approved innovative ideas

schemes.name: Lease Rental Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: 50% of lease rental, capped at Rs 10,000 per month for up to 3 years (as reported)
schemes.description: Reimbursement of a share of office/workspace lease-rental cost to reduce space costs for eligible startups.
schemes.eligibility: Registered eligible startups

schemes.name: Marketing / Advertising Reimbursement
schemes.benefit: Reimbursement of advertising and promotional expenses up to Rs 5 lakh (as reported)
schemes.description: Support toward marketing and promotional expenditure incurred by eligible startups.
schemes.eligibility: Registered eligible startups

schemes.name: Priority Access to WB MSME Venture Capital Fund
schemes.benefit: At least 20% of the fund earmarked for startups (priority equity funding)
schemes.description: Startups get priority equity funding from the West Bengal MSME Venture Capital Fund, with a minimum 20% of the corpus reserved for them.
schemes.eligibility: Bengal-based registered startups

schemes.name: Entrepreneurship Development Centre (EDC) Support
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 10 lakh per university to set up a central EDC
schemes.description: Capital assistance to universities and educational institutions in the state to create a central Entrepreneurship Development Centre and build startup capacity.
schemes.eligibility: Universities and educational institutions in West Bengal

schemes.name: Mentoring Allowance to Institutions
schemes.benefit: Up to Rs 10 lakh per year for mentoring services
schemes.description: Annual assistance to universities, incubation centres, PSUs and R&D institutions for providing mentoring services to startups.
schemes.eligibility: Universities, incubation centres, PSUs, R&D institutions

schemes.name: Fiscal Incentive to Incubators & Investment Bankers
schemes.benefit: Incubators: 2% of investment received by incubates from SEBI-registered VC/PE; Investment bankers: 1% success fee; TBIs: 5% of investments/GoI grants for agri-tech & social enterprises
schemes.description: Ecosystem enablers earn success-fee style incentives for channeling SEBI-registered VC/PE investment into Bengal-based startups.
schemes.eligibility: Registered incubators, TBIs and investment bankers facilitating investment into Bengal startups

schemes.name: Land / Space Allocation in Industrial Estates
schemes.benefit: 20% of available space in industrial estates and SME clusters earmarked for startups
schemes.description: Reserved workspace/land in state industrial estates and SME clusters for startups during their proof-of-concept stage.
schemes.eligibility: Registered startups at proof-of-concept stage

schemes.name: Incubation Fund & Venture Capital Fund (2026 Budget)
schemes.benefit: Rs 40 crore Incubation Fund and Rs 60 crore Venture Capital Fund announced in the 2026 state budget
schemes.description: New funds announced in the West Bengal 2026 budget to support incubation and equity investment, alongside a new startup policy to be notified.
schemes.eligibility: To be notified under the forthcoming new startup policy
